1.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
1.text: Alif-Laam-Meem. (Alphabets of the Arabic language – Allah, and to whomever He reveals, know their precise meanings.)

1.author: Hilali & Khan
1.text: Alif-Lam-Mim. [These letters are one of the miracles of the Quran, and none but Allah (Alone) knows their meanings].

2.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
2.text: The Romans have been defeated (by the Persians)

2.author: Sarwar
2.text: The Romans have been defeated in a nearby land and after this defeat,

2.author: Yusuf Ali
2.text: The Roman Empire has been defeated-

3.author: Ahmed Ali
3.text: In the neighbouring land. But having been conquered they will conquer

3.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
3.text: In the nearby land, and after their defeat they will soon be victorious.

3.author: Arberry
3.text: in the nearer part of the land; and, after their vanquishing, they shall be the victors

3.author: Daryabadi
3.text: In a nearer land; and they, after the overcoming of them, shall soon overcome.

3.author: Hilali & Khan
3.text: In the nearer land (Syria, Iraq, Jordan, and Palestine), and they, after their defeat, will be victorious.

3.author: Itani
3.text: In a nearby territory. But following their defeat, they will be victorious.

3.author: Maududi
3.text: in the neighbouring land; but after their defeat they shall gain victory in a few years.

3.author: Mubarakpuri
3.text: In the nearest land, and they, after their defeat, will be victorious.

3.author: Pickthall
3.text: In the nearer land, and they, after their defeat will be victorious

3.author: Qarai
3.text: in a nearby land, but they, after their defeat, will be victorious

3.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
3.text: in a land close by. But, in a few years after their defeat they shall become the victors.

3.author: Saheeh International
3.text: In the nearest land. But they, after their defeat, will overcome.

3.author: Sarwar
3.text: (within a few years) they will be victorious.

3.author: Shakir
3.text: In a near land, and they, after being vanquished, shall overcome,

3.author: Wahiduddin Khan
3.text: in a nearby land. They will reverse their defeat with a victory

3.author: Yusuf Ali
3.text: In a land close by; but they, (even) after (this) defeat of theirs, will soon be victorious-

3.author: Transliteration
3.text: Fee adna alardi wahum min baAAdi ghalabihim sayaghliboona

4.author: Ahmed Ali
4.text: In a few years (less than ten). God's is the imperative first and last. On that day the believers will rejoice

4.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
4.text: Within a few (up to nine) years time; only for Allah is the command, before and after; and the believers will rejoice on that day.

4.author: Arberry
4.text: in a few years. To God belongs the Command before and after, and on that day the believers shall rejoice

4.author: Daryabadi
4.text: In some few years. Allah's is the command, before and after. And on that day the believers wiil rejoice.

4.author: Hilali & Khan
4.text: Within three to nine years. The decision of the matter, before and after (these events) is only with Allah, (before the defeat of Romans by the Persians, and after, i.e. the defeat of the Persians by the Romans). And on that Day, the believers (i.e. Muslims) will rejoice (at the victory given by Allah to the Romans against the Persians),

4.author: Itani
4.text: In a few years. The matter is up to God, in the past, and in the future. On that day, the believers will rejoice.

4.author: Maududi
4.text: All power belongs to Allah both before and after. On that day will the believers rejoice

4.author: Mubarakpuri
4.text: In Bid`i years. The decision of the matter, before and after is only with Allah. And on that day, the believers will rejoice

4.author: Pickthall
4.text: Within ten years - Allah's is the command in the former case and in the latter - and in that day believers will rejoice

4.author: Qarai
4.text: in a few years. All command belongs to Allah, before this and hereafter, and on that day the faithful will rejoice

4.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
4.text: To Allah belongs the Command before and after, and on that Day the believers will rejoice

4.author: Saheeh International
4.text: Within three to nine years. To Allah belongs the command before and after. And that day the believers will rejoice

4.author: Sarwar
4.text: All matters of the past and future are in the hands of God. The believers will enjoy the help of God on that Day.

4.author: Shakir
4.text: Within a few years. Allah's is the command before and after; and on that day the believers shall rejoice,

4.author: Wahiduddin Khan
4.text: within a few years: [for] with God rests all power of decision, first and last. On that day the believers too will have cause to rejoice,

4.author: Yusuf Ali
4.text: Within a few years. With Allah is the Decision, in the past and in the Future: on that Day shall the Believers rejoice-

4.author: Transliteration
4.text: Fee bidAAi sineena lillahi alamru min qablu wamin baAAdu wayawmaithin yafrahu almuminoona

5.author: Ahmed Ali
5.text: In the help of God. He helps whom He will; He is all-mighty, ever-merciful.

5.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
5.text: With the help of Allah; He helps whomever He wills; and only He is the Almighty, the Most Merciful.

5.author: Arberry
5.text: in God's help; God helps whomsoever He will; and He is the All-mighty, the All-compassionate.

5.author: Daryabadi
5.text: In Allah succour. He succoureth whomsoever He will, and He is the Mighty, the Merciful.

5.author: Hilali & Khan
5.text: With the help of Allah, He helps whom He wills, and He is the All-Mighty, the Most Merciful.

5.author: Itani
5.text: In God’s support. He supports whomever He wills. He is the Almighty, the Merciful.

5.author: Maududi
5.text: at the victory granted by Allah. He grants victory to whomsoever He pleases. He is the Most Mighty, the Most Compassionate.

5.author: Mubarakpuri
5.text: With the help of Allah. He helps whom He wills, and He is the All-Mighty, the Most Merciful.

5.author: Pickthall
5.text: In Allah's help to victory. He helpeth to victory whom He will. He is the Mighty, the Merciful.

5.author: Qarai
5.text: at Allah’s help. He helps whomever He wishes, and He is the All-mighty, the All-merciful.

5.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
5.text: in the victory of Allah. Allah gives victory to whosoever He will, and He is the Mighty, the Merciful.

5.author: Saheeh International
5.text: In the victory of Allah. He gives victory to whom He wills, and He is the Exalted in Might, the Merciful.

5.author: Sarwar
5.text: He helps whomever He wants. He is Majestic and All-merciful.

5.author: Shakir
5.text: With the help of Allah; He helps whom He pleases; and He is the Mighty, the Merciful;

5.author: Wahiduddin Khan
5.text: with the help of God. He helps whom He pleases: He is the Mighty, and the Merciful.

5.author: Yusuf Ali
5.text: With the help of Allah. He helps whom He will, and He is exalted in might, most merciful.

5.author: Transliteration
5.text: Binasri Allahi yansuru man yashao wahuwa alAAazeezu alrraheemu

6.author: Ahmed Ali
6.text: It is a promise of God; and God does not go back on His promise. Yet most men do not understand:

6.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
6.text: The promise of Allah; Allah does not renege on His promise, but most people do not know.

6.author: Arberry
6.text: The promise of God! God fails not His promise, but most men do not know it.

6.author: Daryabadi
6.text: This is Allah's promise; and Allah faileth not His promise. Yet most men know not.

6.author: Hilali & Khan
6.text: (It is) a Promise of Allah (i.e. Allah will give victory to the Romans against the Persians), and Allah fails not in His Promise, but most of men know not.

6.author: Itani
6.text: The promise of God—God never breaks His promise, but most people do not know.

6.author: Maududi
6.text: This is Allah's promise and He does not go back on His promise. But most people do not know.

6.author: Mubarakpuri
6.text: A promise from Allah, and Allah fails not in His promise, but most men know not.

6.author: Pickthall
6.text: It is a promise of Allah. Allah faileth not His promise, but most of mankind know not.

6.author: Qarai
6.text: [This is] a promise of Allah: Allah does not break His promise, but most people do not know.

6.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
6.text: The promise of Allah! Allah does not fail His promise, yet most people do not know.

6.author: Saheeh International
6.text: [It is] the promise of Allah. Allah does not fail in His promise, but most of the people do not know.

6.author: Sarwar
6.text: This is the promise of God. God does not ignore His promise, but many people do not know.

6.author: Shakir
6.text: (This is) Allah's promise! Allah will not fail His promise, but most people do not know.

6.author: Wahiduddin Khan
6.text: [This is] God's promise. Never does God fail to fulfil His promise -- but most people do not know this;

6.author: Yusuf Ali
6.text: (It is) the promise of Allah. Never does Allah depart from His promise: but most men understand not.

6.author: Transliteration
6.text: WaAAda Allahi la yukhlifu Allahu waAAdahu walakinna akthara alnnasi la yaAAlamoona

7.author: Ahmed Ali
7.text: They only know the palpable life of this world, and are oblivious of the Hereafter.

7.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
7.text: They know only the visible worldly life; and are totally neglectful of the Hereafter.

7.author: Arberry
7.text: They know an outward part of the present life, but of the Hereafter they are heedless.

7.author: Daryabadi
7.text: They know only the outward appearance of the life of the world, and they! of the Hereafter, they are neglecetful.

7.author: Hilali & Khan
7.text: They know only the outside appearance of the life of the world (i.e. the matters of their livelihood, like irrigating or sowing or reaping, etc.), and they are heedless of the Hereafter.

7.author: Itani
7.text: They know an outer aspect of the worldly life, but they are heedless of the Hereafter.

7.author: Maududi
7.text: People simply know the outward aspect of the worldly life but are utterly heedless of the Hereafter.

7.author: Mubarakpuri
7.text: They know only the outer appearance of the life of the world, and they are heedless of the Hereafter.

7.author: Pickthall
7.text: They know only some appearance of the life of the world, and are heedless of the Hereafter.

7.author: Qarai
7.text: They know just an outward aspect of the life of the world, but they are oblivious of the Hereafter.

7.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
7.text: They know an outward part of this life, but of the Everlasting Life they are inattentive.

7.author: Saheeh International
7.text: They know what is apparent of the worldly life, but they, of the Hereafter, are unaware.

7.author: Sarwar
7.text: They only know the superficial realities of the worldly life and they are unaware of the life to come.

7.author: Shakir
7.text: They know the outward of this world's life, but of the hereafter they are absolutely heedless.

7.author: Wahiduddin Khan
7.text: they only know the outward appearance of the life of this world, and they are neglectful of the Hereafter.

7.author: Yusuf Ali
7.text: They know but the outer (things) in the life of this world: but of the End of things they are heedless.

7.author: Transliteration
7.text: YaAAlamoona thahiran mina alhayati alddunya wahum AAani alakhirati hum ghafiloona

8.author: Ahmed Ali
8.text: Do they not think for themselves that God did not create the heavens and the earth and all that lies between them without reason and a determined purpose? But many men reject the meeting with their Lord.

8.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
8.text: Have they not inwardly pondered – that Allah has not created the heavens and the earth, and all that is between them, except with truth and a fixed term? And indeed many people deny the meeting with their Lord.

8.author: Arberry
8.text: What, have they not considered within themselves? God created not the heavens and the earth, and what between them is, save with the truth and a stated term; yet most men disbelieve in the encounter with their Lord.

8.author: Daryabadi
8.text: Ponder they not in their minds: Allah hath not created the heavens and the earth and whatsoever is in-between the twain save with a purpose and for a term appointed? And verily many men in the meecing of their Lord are unbelievers.

8.author: Hilali & Khan
8.text: Do they not think deeply (in their ownselves) about themselves (how Allah created them from nothing, and similarly He will resurrect them)? Allah has created not the heavens and the earth, and all that is between them, except with truth and for an appointed term. And indeed many of mankind deny the Meeting with their Lord. [See Tafsir At-Tabari, Part 21, Page 24].

8.author: Itani
8.text: Do they not reflect within themselves? God did not create the heavens and the earth, and what is between them, except with reason, and for a specific duration. But most people, regarding meeting their Lord, are disbelievers.

8.author: Maududi
8.text: Do they not reflect on themselves? Allah created the heavens and the earth and whatever lies between them in Truth and for an appointed term. Yet many people deny that they will meet their Lord.

8.author: Mubarakpuri
8.text: (Do they not reflect upon themselves) Thinking and pondering how Allah created various things in the upper and lower realms and in the space between, realizing that this was not created in jest or in vain, but in truth, and that it will continue until an appointed time, the Day of Resurrection

8.author: Pickthall
8.text: Have they not pondered upon themselves? Allah created not the heavens and the earth, and that which is between them, save with truth and for a destined end. But truly many of mankind are disbelievers in the meeting with their Lord.

8.author: Qarai
8.text: Have they not reflected in their own souls? Allah did not create the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them except with consummate wisdom and for a specified term. Indeed many people disbelieve in the encounter with their Lord.

8.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
8.text: Have they never thought to themselves that Allah did not create the heavens and the earth and all that is between except with truth, and for a stated term? Yet most people disbelieve that they will ever meet their Lord.

8.author: Saheeh International
8.text: Do they not contemplate within themselves? Allah has not created the heavens and the earth and what is between them except in truth and for a specified term. And indeed, many of the people, in [the matter of] the meeting with their Lord, are disbelievers.

8.author: Sarwar
8.text: Have they not thought that God has not created the heavens and the earth and all that is between them but for a genuine purpose to exist for an appointed term? Many people do not believe in their meeting with their Lord.

8.author: Shakir
8.text: Do they not reflect within themselves: Allah did not create the heavens and the earth and what is between them two but with truth, and (for) an appointed term? And most surely most of the people are deniers of the meeting of their Lord.

8.author: Wahiduddin Khan
8.text: Do they not ponder about their own selves? God has created the heavens and the earth and all that is between them for a purpose and for an appointed time? Yet many deny they will ever meet with their Lord.

8.author: Yusuf Ali
8.text: Do they not reflect in their own minds? Not but for just ends and for a term appointed, did Allah create the heavens and the earth, and all between them: yet are there truly many among men who deny the meeting with their Lord (at the Resurrection)!

8.author: Transliteration
8.text: Awalam yatafakkaroo fee anfusihim ma khalaqa Allahu alssamawati waalarda wama baynahuma illa bialhaqqi waajalin musamman wainna katheeran mina alnnasi biliqai rabbihim lakafiroona

9.author: Ahmed Ali
9.text: Have they not travelled on the earth and seen how the others before them had met their end? They were far more powerful than them, furrowed the earth and colonised it far more than they; and their apostles came to them with visible signs. It was surely not for God to wrong them, they wronged themselves.

9.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
9.text: And did they not travel in the land to see what sort of fate befell those before them? They were stronger than them, and they cultivated the land and inhabited it more than them, and their Noble Messengers came to them with clear proofs; so it did not befit Allah’s Majesty to oppress them, but it was they who used to wrong themselves.

9.author: Arberry
9.text: What, have they not journeyed in the land and beheld how was the end of those before them? They were stronger than themselves in might, and they ploughed up the earth and cultivated it more than they themselves have cultivated it; and their Messengers came to them with the clear signs; and God would never wrong them, but themselves they wronged.

9.author: Daryabadi
9.text: Have they not journeyed in the land and observed what wise hath been the end of those before them? They were stronger than they in power and they brake up the earth and they inhabited it with greater affluence than these have inhabited it, and their apostles came unto them with evidences. And Allah was not one to wrong them, but themselves they were wont to wrong.

9.author: Hilali & Khan
9.text: Do they not travel in the land, and see what was the end of those before them? They were superior to them in strength, and they tilled the earth and populated it in greater numbers than these (pagans) have done, and there came to them their Messengers with clear proofs. Surely, Allah wronged them not, but they used to wrong themselves.

9.author: Itani
9.text: Have they not travelled the earth and seen how those before them ended up? They were more powerful than them, and they cultivated the land and developed it more than they developed it, and their messengers came to them with clear signs. God would never wrong them, but they used to wrong themselves.

9.author: Maududi
9.text: Have they not travelled through the earth that they may observe what was the end of their predecessors who were far mightier and tilled the land and built upon it more than these have ever built? And Allah's Messengers came to them with Clear Signs. It was not Allah Who wronged them, but it is they who wronged themselves.

9.author: Mubarakpuri
9.text: Have they not traveled through the earth and observed how was the end of those before them? They were greater than them in power, and they plowed the earth and built it up more than they have built it up, and their messengers came to them with clear evidences. And Allah would not ever have wronged them, but they were wronging themselves.

9.author: Pickthall
9.text: Have they not travelled in the land and seen the nature of the consequence for those who were before them? They were stronger than these in power, and they dug the earth and built upon it more than these have built. Messengers of their own came unto them with clear proofs (of Allah's Sovereignty). Surely Allah wronged them not, but they did wrong themselves.

9.author: Qarai
9.text: Have they not travelled in the land and seen how was the fate of those who were before them? They were more powerful than them, and they plowed the earth and developed it more than they have developed it. Their apostles brought them manifest proofs. So it was not Allah who wronged them, but it was they who used to wrong themselves.

9.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
9.text: What, have they never journeyed in the land and seen what was the end of those before them? They were stronger in might than themselves, and they plowed the land and cultivated it more than they themselves have cultivated it. And to them, their Messengers came with clear signs, and Allah did not wrong them, but they wronged themselves.

9.author: Saheeh International
9.text: Have they not traveled through the earth and observed how was the end of those before them? They were greater than them in power, and they plowed the earth and built it up more than they have built it up, and their messengers came to them with clear evidences. And Allah would not ever have wronged them, but they were wronging themselves.

9.author: Sarwar
9.text: Have they not travelled through the land to see how terrible was the end of the people who lived before them. The people who lived before them were stronger than them in might, in tilling, and in developing the earth. Our Messengers came to them with clear miracles. God did not do an injustice to them but they wronged themselves.

9.author: Shakir
9.text: Have they not travelled in the earth and seen how was the end of those before them? They were stronger than these in prowess, and dug up the earth, and built on it in greater abundance than these have built on it, and there came to them their apostles with clear arguments; so it was not beseeming for Allah that He should deal with them unjustly, but they dealt unjustly with their own souls.

9.author: Wahiduddin Khan
9.text: Have they not travelled through the land and seen what end their predecessors met? They were mightier than them: they cultivated the earth more and built more upon it than these have ever built. Their own messengers also came to them with clear signs: God did not wrong them; they wronged themselves.

9.author: Yusuf Ali
9.text: Do they not travel through the earth, and see what was the end of those before them? They were superior to them in strength: they tilled the soil and populated it in greater numbers than these have done: there came to them their messengers with Clear (Signs). (Which they rejected, to their own destruction): It was not Allah Who wronged them, but they wronged their own souls.

9.author: Transliteration
9.text: Awalam yaseeroo fee alardi fayanthuroo kayfa kana AAaqibatu allatheena min qablihim kanoo ashadda minhum quwwatan waatharoo alarda waAAamarooha akthara mimma AAamarooha wajaathum rusuluhum bialbayyinati fama kana Allahu liyathlimahum walakin kanoo anfusahum yathlimoona

10.author: Ahmed Ali
10.text: Therefore evil was the end of those who did evil, for they denied the signs of God and made fun of them.

10.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
10.text: Then the fate of those who committed evil to the extreme was that they denied the signs of Allah and used to mock at them.

10.author: Arberry
10.text: Then the end of those that did evil was evil, for that they cried lies to the signs of God and mocked at them.

10.author: Daryabadi
10.text: Then the end of those who committed evil was evil, for they belied the signs of Allah and they were wont to mock at them.

10.author: Hilali & Khan
10.text: Then evil was the end of those who did evil, because they belied the Ayat (proofs, evidences, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, Messengers, etc.) of Allah and made mock of them.

10.author: Itani
10.text: Then, evil was the end of those who committed evil. That is because they rejected God’s revelations, and used to ridicule them.

10.author: Maududi
10.text: Evil was the end of those evil-doers, for they gave the lie to Allah's Signs and scoffed at them.

10.author: Mubarakpuri
10.text: Then evil was the end of those who did evil, because they denied the Ayat of Allah and made a mockery of them.

10.author: Pickthall
10.text: Then evil was the consequence to those who dealt in evil, because they denied the revelations of Allah and made a mock of them.

10.author: Qarai
10.text: Then the fate of those who committed misdeeds was that they denied the signs of Allah and they used to deride them.

10.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
10.text: Evil was the end of the harmdoers, because they belied the verses of Allah and mocked at them.

10.author: Saheeh International
10.text: Then the end of those who did evil was the worst [consequence] because they denied the signs of Allah and used to ridicule them.

10.author: Sarwar
10.text: The end of the evil-doers was terrible, for they had rejected the revelations of God and mocked them.

10.author: Shakir
10.text: Then evil was the end of those who did evil, because they | rejected the communications of Allah and used to mock them.

10.author: Wahiduddin Khan
10.text: Then the end of those who committed evil was evil, for they belied the signs of God, and they derided them.

10.author: Yusuf Ali
10.text: In the long run evil in the extreme will be the End of those who do evil; for that they rejected the Signs of Allah, and held them up to ridicule.

10.author: Transliteration
10.text: Thumma kana AAaqibata allatheena asaoo alssooa an kaththaboo biayati Allahi wakanoo biha yastahzioona

11.author: Ahmed Ali
11.text: God originates creation, and then will revert it, then you will go back to Him.

11.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
11.text: Allah initiates the creation, then will create it again – then it is to Him that you will return.

11.author: Arberry
11.text: God originates creation, then brings it back again, then unto Him you shall be returned.

11.author: Daryabadi
11.text: Allah originateth the creation, then He shall restore it, then unto Him ye shall be returned.

11.author: Hilali & Khan
11.text: Allah (Alone) originates the creation, then He will repeat it, then to Him you will be returned.

11.author: Itani
11.text: God originates creation, and then repeats it. Then to Him you will be returned.

11.author: Maududi
11.text: Allah creates in the first instance and will later repeat it. Thereafter it is to Him that you shall be sent back.

11.author: Mubarakpuri
11.text: Allah originates the creation, then He will repeat it, then to Him you will be returned.

11.author: Pickthall
11.text: Allah produceth creation, then He reproduceth it, then unto Him ye will be returned.

11.author: Qarai
11.text: Allah originates the creation, then He will bring it back, then you will be brought back to Him.

11.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
11.text: Allah originates creation, then brings it back again, then to Him you shall be returned.

11.author: Saheeh International
11.text: Allah begins creation; then He will repeat it; then to Him you will be returned.

11.author: Sarwar
11.text: God begins the creation then causes it to turn back and to Him you will all return.

11.author: Shakir
11.text: Allah originates the creation, then reproduces it, then to Him you shall be brought back.

11.author: Wahiduddin Khan
11.text: God originates the creation, and shall repeat it, then to Him you shall be returned.

11.author: Yusuf Ali
11.text: It is Allah Who begins (the process of) creation; then repeats it; then shall ye be brought back to Him.

11.author: Transliteration
11.text: Allahu yabdao alkhalqa thumma yuAAeeduhu thumma ilayhi turjaAAoona

12.author: Ahmed Ali
12.text: The day the Resurrection comes the sinners will be overwhelmed with despair.

12.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
12.text: And on the Last Day– when it is established, the guilty* will lose all hope. (* The disbelievers.)

12.author: Arberry
12.text: Upon the day when the Hour is come, the sinners shall be confounded

12.author: Daryabadi
12.text: And on the day whereon the Hour arriveth, dumb-struck will be the culprits.

12.author: Hilali & Khan
12.text: And on the Day when the Hour will be established, the Mujrimun (disbelievers, sinners, criminals, polytheists, etc.) will be plunged into destruction with deep regrets, sorrows, and despair.

12.author: Itani
12.text: On the Day when the Hour takes place, the guilty will despair.

12.author: Maududi
12.text: On that Day when the Hour will come to pass, the criminals shall be dumbfounded.

12.author: Mubarakpuri
12.text: And on the Day when the Hour will be established, the criminals will be plunged into destruction with despair.

12.author: Pickthall
12.text: And in the day when the Hour riseth the unrighteous will despair.

12.author: Qarai
12.text: And when the Hour sets in, the guilty will despair.

12.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
12.text: On the Day when the Hour comes, the wrongdoers will be speechless.

12.author: Saheeh International
12.text: And the Day the Hour appears the criminals will be in despair.

12.author: Sarwar
12.text: On the day when the Hour of Doom comes, the criminals will despair.

12.author: Shakir
12.text: And at the time when the hour shall come the guilty shall be in despair.

12.author: Wahiduddin Khan
12.text: On the Day the Hour arrives, the guilty will be struck dumb with despair,

12.author: Yusuf Ali
12.text: On the Day that the Hour will be established, the guilty will be struck dumb with despair.

13.author: Ahmed Ali
13.text: No intercessor will they have among those they associated (with God), and will reject their partners.

13.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
13.text: And the partners they ascribed will not be their intercessors – and they will deny their partners.

13.author: Arberry
13.text: no intercessors shall they have amongst their associates, and they shall disbelieve in their associates.

13.author: Daryabadi
13.text: And not from their associate-gods, there will be intercessors for them, and unto their associate-gods they will be unbelievers.

13.author: Hilali & Khan
13.text: No intercessor will they have from those whom they made equal with Allah (partners i.e. their so-called associate gods), and they will (themselves) reject and deny their partners.

13.author: Itani
13.text: They will have no intercessors from among their idols, and they will disown their partners.

13.author: Maududi
13.text: None whom they had associated with Allah in His Divinity will intercede on their behalf; rather, they will disown those whom they had set up as Allah's associates in His Divinity.

13.author: Mubarakpuri
13.text: No intercessors will they have from those whom they made equal with Allah, and they will reject and deny their partners.

13.author: Pickthall
13.text: There will be none to intercede for them of those whom they made equal with Allah. And they will reject their partners (whom they ascribed unto Him).

13.author: Qarai
13.text: None of those whom they ascribed as partners [to Allah] will intercede for them, and they will disavow their partners.

13.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
13.text: They shall have none to intercede for them amongst their associates, and they shall disbelieve in their associates.

13.author: Saheeh International
13.text: And there will not be for them among their [alleged] partners any intercessors, and they will [then] be disbelievers in their partners.

13.author: Sarwar
13.text: None of the idols will intercede for them and they will reject their idols.

13.author: Shakir
13.text: And they shall not have any intercessors from among their gods they have joined with Allah, and they shall be deniers of their associate-gods.

13.author: Wahiduddin Khan
13.text: and they will have no intercessors among those partners they ascribed to God. They will deny these partners.

13.author: Yusuf Ali
13.text: No intercessor will they have among their "Partners" and they will (themselves) reject their "Partners".

13.author: Transliteration
13.text: Walam yakun lahum min shurakaihim shufaAAao wakanoo bishurakaihim kafireena

14.author: Ahmed Ali
14.text: The day the Hour comes they will be separated into categories.

14.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
14.text: And on the Last Day – that day they will all separate.

14.author: Arberry
14.text: Upon the day when the Hour is come, that day they shall be divided;

14.author: Daryabadi
14.text: On the Day whereon the Hour arriveth, that Day they shall be separated.

14.author: Hilali & Khan
14.text: And on the Day when the Hour will be established, that Day shall (all men) be separated (i.e. the believers will be separated from the disbelievers).

14.author: Itani
14.text: On the Day when the Hour takes place—on that Day they will separate.

14.author: Maududi
14.text: On that Day when the Hour will come to pass, people will be split into groups.

14.author: Mubarakpuri
14.text: And on the Day when the Hour will be established -- that Day shall they be separated.

14.author: Pickthall
14.text: In the day when the Hour cometh, in that day they will be sundered.

14.author: Qarai
14.text: The day the Hour sets in, they will be divided on that day [in separate groups]:

14.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
14.text: On the Day when the Hour has come, they will be divided,

14.author: Saheeh International
14.text: And the Day the Hour appears - that Day they will become separated.

14.author: Sarwar
14.text: When that day comes, (people) will be separated from one another.

14.author: Shakir
14.text: And at the time when the hour shall come, at that time they shall become separated one from the other.

14.author: Wahiduddin Khan
14.text: When the Last Hour dawns -- on that Day they will be sorted out:

14.author: Yusuf Ali
14.text: On the Day that the Hour will be established,- that Day shall (all men) be sorted out.

15.author: Ahmed Ali
15.text: Those who believed and did the right will be feasted in a rich, well-watered meadow.

15.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
15.text: So those who believed and did good deeds – they will be hosted in the Garden.

15.author: Arberry
15.text: as for those who believed, and did deeds of righteousness, they shall walk with joy in a green meadow,

15.author: Daryabadi
15.text: Then as for those who believed and worked righteous works, they shall be in a meadow made happy.

15.author: Hilali & Khan
15.text: Then as for those who believed (in the Oneness of Allah Islamic Monotheism) and did righteous good deeds, such shall be honoured and made to enjoy luxurious life (forever) in a Garden of delight (Paradise).

15.author: Itani
15.text: As for those who believed and did good deeds—they will be delighted in meadows.

15.author: Maududi
15.text: Then those who believed and acted righteously will be placed in a Garden and will be happy and jubilant.

15.author: Mubarakpuri
15.text: Then as for those who believed and did righteous good deeds, such shall be honored and made to enjoy a luxurious life in a Garden of Delight.

15.author: Pickthall
15.text: As for those who believed and did good works, they will be made happy in a Garden.

15.author: Qarai
15.text: As for those who have faith and do righteous deeds, they shall be in a garden, rejoicing.

15.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
15.text: those who believe and did good deeds shall be well pleased in a garden,

15.author: Saheeh International
15.text: And as for those who had believed and done righteous deeds, they will be in a garden [of Paradise], delighted.

15.author: Sarwar
15.text: The righteously striving believers will happily live in paradise.

15.author: Shakir
15.text: Then as to those who believed and did good, they shall be made happy in a garden.

15.author: Wahiduddin Khan
15.text: those who believed and did good deeds will rejoice in a Garden,

15.author: Yusuf Ali
15.text: Then those who have believed and worked righteous deeds, shall be made happy in a Mead of Delight.

15.author: Transliteration
15.text: Faamma allatheena amanoo waAAamiloo alssalihati fahum fee rawdatin yuhbaroona

16.author: Ahmed Ali
16.text: Those who did not believe and rejected Our signs and the meeting in the Hereafter, will be given over to punishment.

16.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
16.text: And those who disbelieved, and denied Our signs and confronting the Hereafter – they will be hauled into the punishment.

16.author: Arberry
16.text: but as for those who disbelieved, and cried lies to Our signs and the encounter of the Hereafter, they shall be arraigned into the chastisement.

16.author: Daryabadi
16.text: And as for those who disbelieved and belied Our signs and the meeting of the Hereafter--these to the torment shall be brought.

16.author: Hilali & Khan
16.text: And as for those who disbelieved and belied Our Ayat (proofs, evidences, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, Allah's Messengers, Resurrection, etc.), and the Meeting of the Hereafter, such shall be brought forth to the torment (in the Hell-fire).

16.author: Itani
16.text: But as for those who disbelieved, and rejected Our signs and the encounter of the Hereafter—those will be hauled into the torment.

16.author: Maududi
16.text: As for those who disbelieved and gave the lie to Our Signs and to the encounter of the Hereafter, they will be arraigned for chastisement.

16.author: Mubarakpuri
16.text: And as for those who disbelieved and denied Our Ayat, and the meeting of the Hereafter, such shall be brought forth to the torment.

16.author: Pickthall
16.text: But as for those who disbelieved and denied Our revelations, and denied the meeting of the Hereafter, such will be brought to doom.

16.author: Qarai
16.text: But as for those who were faithless and denied Our signs and the encounter of the Hereafter, they will be brought to the punishment.

16.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
16.text: but those who disbelieved and belied Our verses and the meeting of the Everlasting Life, shall be arraigned for punishment.

16.author: Saheeh International
16.text: But as for those who disbelieved and denied Our verses and the meeting of the Hereafter, those will be brought into the punishment [to remain].

16.author: Sarwar
16.text: However, the disbelievers, who called Our revelations and the Day of Judgment lies, will be brought into torment.

16.author: Shakir
16.text: And as to those who disbelieved and rejected Our communications and the meeting of the hereafter, these shall be brought over to the chastisement.

16.author: Wahiduddin Khan
16.text: and as for those who denied the truth and belied Our signs and the meeting in the Hereafter -- they shall be brought to the torment.

16.author: Yusuf Ali
16.text: And those who have rejected Faith and falsely denied our Signs and the meeting of the Hereafter,- such shall be brought forth to Punishment.

16.author: Transliteration
16.text: Waamma allatheena kafaroo wakaththaboo biayatina waliqai alakhirati faolaika fee alAAathabi muhdaroona

17.author: Ahmed Ali
17.text: So extol God when the evening comes and the day dawns,

17.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
17.text: So proclaim the Purity of Allah when you witness the night and the morning.

17.author: Arberry
17.text: So glory be to God both in your evening hour and in your morning hour.

17.author: Daryabadi
17.text: So hallow Allah when ye enter the night and when ye enter the morning, --

17.author: Hilali & Khan
17.text: So glorify Allah [above all that (evil) they associate with Him (O believers)], when you come up to the evening [i.e. offer the (Maghrib) sunset and ('Isha') night prayers], and when you enter the morning [i.e. offer the (Fajr) morning prayer].

17.author: Itani
17.text: So glorify God when you retire at night, and when you rise in the morning.

17.author: Maududi
17.text: So glorify Allah in the evening and the morning.

17.author: Mubarakpuri
17.text: So glorify Allah, when you enter the evening, and when you enter the morning.

17.author: Pickthall
17.text: So glory be to Allah when ye enter the night and when ye enter the morning -

17.author: Qarai
17.text: So glorify Allah when you enter evening and when you rise at dawn.

17.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
17.text: Therefore, exalt Allah when you enter the evening and in the morning.

17.author: Saheeh International
17.text: So exalted is Allah when you reach the evening and when you reach the morning.

17.author: Sarwar
17.text: Glory belongs to God all the time, in the evening and in the morning.

17.author: Shakir
17.text: Therefore glory be to Allah when you enter upon the time of the evening and when you enter upon the time of the morning.

17.author: Wahiduddin Khan
17.text: So glorify God in the evening and in the morning,

17.author: Yusuf Ali
17.text: So (give) glory to Allah, when ye reach eventide and when ye rise in the morning;

17.author: Transliteration
17.text: Fasubhana Allahi heena tumsoona waheena tusbihoona

18.author: Ahmed Ali
18.text: For His is the praise in the heavens and the earth, -- and at nightfall and the time of noon.

18.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
18.text: And only His is the praise in the heavens and the earth – and before the day ends and at noon.

18.author: Arberry
18.text: His is the praise in the heavens and earth, alike at the setting sun and in your noontide hour.

18.author: Daryabadi
18.text: And His is all praise in the heavens and the earth! --and at the sun's decline and when ye enter the noon.

18.author: Hilali & Khan
18.text: And His is all the praises and thanks in the heavens and the earth, and (glorify Him) in the afternoon (i.e. offer 'Asr prayer) and when you come up to the time, when the day begins to decline (i.e. offer Zuhr prayer). (Ibn 'Abbas said: "These are the five compulsory congregational prayers mentioned in the Quran)."

18.author: Itani
18.text: His is the praise in the heavens and on earth, and in the evening, and when you reach midday.

18.author: Maududi
18.text: His is all praise in the heavens and in the earth; (and glorify Him) in the afternoon and when the sun begins to decline.

18.author: Mubarakpuri
18.text: And His is all the praise in the heavens and the earth; and in `Ashiyya and when Tuzhirun.

18.author: Pickthall
18.text: Unto Him be praise in the heavens and the earth! - and at the sun's decline and in the noonday.

18.author: Qarai
18.text: To Him belongs all praise in the heavens and the earth, at nightfall and when you enter noontime.

18.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
18.text: His is the praise in the heavens and the earth, at the setting sun and at noon.

18.author: Saheeh International
18.text: And to Him is [due all] praise throughout the heavens and the earth. And [exalted is He] at night and when you are at noon.

18.author: Sarwar
18.text: To Him belongs all the thanks giving which takes place in the heavens and the earth, in the evenings and the noontime.

18.author: Shakir
18.text: And to Him belongs praise in the heavens and the earth, and at nightfall and when you are at midday.

18.author: Wahiduddin Khan
18.text: and praise be to Him in the heavens and on the earth, and glorify Him in the late afternoon, and at midday.

18.author: Yusuf Ali
18.text: Yea, to Him be praise, in the heavens and on earth; and in the late afternoon and when the day begins to decline.

18.author: Transliteration
18.text: Walahu alhamdu fee alssamawati waalardi waAAashiyyan waheena tuthhiroona

19.author: Ahmed Ali
19.text: He brings the living from the dead, the dead from the living, and quickens the earth after it had died. So will you be brought forth (from the dead).

19.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
19.text: He brings forth the living from the dead, and brings forth the dead from the living, and He revives the earth after its death; and this how you will be raised.

19.author: Arberry
19.text: He brings forth the living from the dead, and brings forth the dead from the living, and He revives the earth after it is dead; even so you shall be brought forth.

19.author: Daryabadi
19.text: He bringeth forth the living from the lifeless, and He bringeth forth the lifeless from the living, and He quickeneth the earth after the death thereof; and even so shall ye be brought forth.

19.author: Hilali & Khan
19.text: He brings out the living from the dead, and brings out the dead from the living. And He revives the earth after its death. And thus shall you be brought out (resurrected).

19.author: Itani
19.text: He brings the living out of the dead, and He brings the dead out of the living, and He revives the land after it had died. Likewise you will be resurrected.

19.author: Maududi
19.text: He brings forth the living from the dead and brings forth the dead from the living, and revives the earth after it is dead. Likewise will you be raised to life (after you die).

19.author: Mubarakpuri
19.text: He brings out the living from the dead, and brings out the dead from the living. And He revives the earth after its death. And thus shall you be brought out.

19.author: Pickthall
19.text: He bringeth forth the living from the dead, and He bringeth forth the dead from the living, and He reviveth the earth after her death. And even so will ye be brought forth.

19.author: Qarai
19.text: He brings forth the living from the dead, and brings forth the dead from the living, and revives the earth after its death. Likewise, you [too] shall be raised [from the dead].

19.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
19.text: He brings out the living from the dead, and the dead from the living. He revives the earth after its death. Likewise you shall be brought forth.

19.author: Saheeh International
19.text: He brings the living out of the dead and brings the dead out of the living and brings to life the earth after its lifelessness. And thus will you be brought out.

19.author: Sarwar
19.text: He brings forth the living from the dead, takes out the dead from the living, and revives the earth from its death. Thus, you will all be brought back to life again.

19.author: Shakir
19.text: He brings forth the living from the dead and brings forth the dead from the living, and gives life to the earth after its death, and thus shall you be brought forth.

19.author: Wahiduddin Khan
19.text: He brings forth the living from the dead and the dead from the living. He gives life to the earth after its death, and you shall be raised to life in the same way.

19.author: Yusuf Ali
19.text: It is He Who brings out the living from the dead, and brings out the dead from the living, and Who gives life to the earth after it is dead: and thus shall ye be brought out (from the dead).

19.author: Transliteration
19.text: Yukhriju alhayya mina almayyiti wayukhriju almayyita mina alhayyi wayuhyee alarda baAAda mawtiha wakathalika tukhrajoona

20.author: Ahmed Ali
20.text: Among His signs is that He created you from the earth, and you are now human beings dispersed everywhere.

20.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
20.text: And among His signs is that He created you from dust, and therefore you are human beings spread in the earth.

20.author: Arberry
20.text: And of His signs is that He created you of dust; then lo, you are mortals, all scattered abroad.

20.author: Daryabadi
20.text: And of His signs is, that he created you of dust, then lo! ye are humankind spreading yourselves.

20.author: Hilali & Khan
20.text: And among His Signs is this, that He created you (Adam) from dust, and then [Hawwa' (Eve) from Adam's rib, and then his offspring from the semen, and], - behold you are human beings scattered!

20.author: Itani
20.text: And of His signs is that He created you from dust; and behold, you become humans spreading out.

20.author: Maududi
20.text: And of His Signs is that He created you from dust and behold, you became human beings, and are multiplying around (the earth).

20.author: Mubarakpuri
20.text: And among His signs is this that He created you from dust, and then -- behold you are human beings scattered!

20.author: Pickthall
20.text: And of His signs is this: He created you of dust, and behold you human beings, ranging widely!

20.author: Qarai
20.text: Of His signs is that He created you from dust, then, behold, you are humans scattering [all over]!

20.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
20.text: And of His signs is that He created you from dust and you became humans scattered throughout the earth.

20.author: Saheeh International
20.text: And of His signs is that He created you from dust; then, suddenly you were human beings dispersing [throughout the earth].

20.author: Sarwar
20.text: Some evidence of His existence are His creating you from clay and from that you became human beings scattered all around;

20.author: Shakir
20.text: And one of His signs is that He created you from dust, then lo! you are mortals (who) scatter.

20.author: Wahiduddin Khan
20.text: One of His signs is that He created you from dust and, behold, you became human beings and multiplied yourselves throughout the earth.

20.author: Yusuf Ali
20.text: Among His Signs in this, that He created you from dust; and then,- behold, ye are men scattered (far and wide)!

20.author: Transliteration
20.text: Wamin ayatihi an khalaqakum min turabin thumma itha antum basharun tantashiroona

21.author: Ahmed Ali
21.text: Another of His signs is that He created mates of your own kind of yourselves so that you may get peace of mind from them, and has put love and compassion between you. Verily there are signs in this for those who reflect.

21.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
21.text: And among His signs is that He created spouses for you from yourselves for you to gain rest from them, and kept love and mercy between yourselves; indeed in this are signs for the people who ponder.

21.author: Arberry
21.text: And of His signs is that He created for you, of yourselves, spouses, that you might repose in them, and He has set between you love and mercy. Surely in that are signs for a people who consider.

21.author: Daryabadi
21.text: And of His signs is, thathe created for you from yourselves pouses that ye may find repose in them, and He set between you affection and mercy. verily herein are signs for a people who ponder.

21.author: Hilali & Khan
21.text: And among His Signs is this, that He created for you wives from among yourselves, that you may find repose in them, and He has put between you affection and mercy. Verily, in that are indeed signs for a people who reflect.

21.author: Itani
21.text: And of His signs is that He created for you mates from among yourselves, so that you may find tranquility in them; and He planted love and compassion between you. In this are signs for people who reflect.

21.author: Maududi
21.text: And of His Signs is that He has created mates for you from your own kind that you may find peace in them and He has set between you love and mercy. Surely there are Signs in this for those who reflect.

21.author: Mubarakpuri
21.text: And among His signs is this that He created for you wives from among yourselves, that you may find repose in them, and He has put between you affection and mercy. Verily, in that are indeed signs for a people who reflect.

21.author: Pickthall
21.text: And of His signs is this: He created for you helpmeets from yourselves that ye might find rest in them, and He ordained between you love and mercy. Lo! herein indeed are portents for folk who reflect.

21.author: Qarai
21.text: And of His signs is that He created for you mates from your own selves that you may take comfort in them, and He ordained affection and mercy between you. There are indeed signs in that for a people who reflect.

21.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
21.text: And of His signs is that He created for you wives from among yourselves, that you might reside with them, and has put kindness and mercy between you. Surely, there are signs in this for those who think.

21.author: Saheeh International
21.text: And of His signs is that He created for you from yourselves mates that you may find tranquillity in them; and He placed between you affection and mercy. Indeed in that are signs for a people who give thought.

21.author: Sarwar
21.text: His creating spouses for you out of yourselves so that you might take comfort in them and His creating love and mercy among you. In this there is evidence (of the truth) for the people who (carefully) think.

21.author: Shakir
21.text: And one of His signs is that He created mates for you from yourselves that you may find rest in them, and He put between you love and compassion; most surely there are signs in this for a people who reflect.

21.author: Wahiduddin Khan
21.text: Another of His signs is that He created for you from among yourselves spouses, so that you might find repose in them, and He created between you affection and kindness. Truly there are signs in this for people who reflect.

21.author: Yusuf Ali
21.text: And among His Signs is this, that He created for you mates from among yourselves, that ye may dwell in tranquillity with them, and He has put love and mercy between your (hearts): verily in that are Signs for those who reflect.

21.author: Transliteration
21.text: Wamin ayatihi an khalaqa lakum min anfusikum azwajan litaskunoo ilayha wajaAAala baynakum mawaddatan warahmatan inna fee thalika laayatin liqawmin yatafakkaroona

22.author: Ahmed Ali
22.text: Among other signs of His is the creation of the heavens and the earth, and the variety of your tongues and complexions. Surely there are signs in this for those who understand.

22.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
22.text: And among His signs is the creation of the heavens and the earth, and the differences in your languages and colours; indeed in this are signs for people who know.

22.author: Arberry
22.text: And of His signs is the creation of the heavens and earth and the variety of your tongues and hues. Surely in that are signs for all living beings.

22.author: Daryabadi
22.text: And of His signs are the creation of the heavens and the earth, and the variation of your languages and complexions; verily herein are signs for men of knowledge.

22.author: Hilali & Khan
22.text: And among His Signs is the creation of the heavens and the earth, and the difference of your languages and colours. Verily, in that are indeed signs for men of sound knowledge.

22.author: Itani
22.text: And of His signs is the creation of the heavens and the earth, and the diversity of your languages and colors. In this are signs for those who know.

22.author: Maududi
22.text: And of His Signs is the creation of the heavens and the earth and the diversity of your tongues and colours. Indeed there are Signs in this for the wise.

22.author: Mubarakpuri
22.text: And among His signs is the creation of the heavens and the earth, and the difference of your languages and colors. Verily, in that are indeed signs for men of sound knowledge.

22.author: Pickthall
22.text: And of His signs is the creation of the heavens and the earth, and the difference of your languages and colours. Lo! herein indeed are portents for men of knowledge.

22.author: Qarai
22.text: Among His signs is the creation of the heavens and the earth, and the difference of your languages and colours. There are indeed signs in that for those who know.

22.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
22.text: And His signs are the creation of heavens and earth and the diversity of your tongues and colors. Surely, there are signs in this for all the worlds.

22.author: Saheeh International
22.text: And of His signs is the creation of the heavens and the earth and the diversity of your languages and your colors. Indeed in that are signs for those of knowledge.

22.author: Sarwar
22.text: Other evidence of His existence are the creation of the heavens and the earth and the differences of languages and colors. In this there is evidence (of the truth) for the worlds (mankind).

22.author: Shakir
22.text: And one of His signs is the creation of the heavens and the earth and the diversity of your tongues and colors; most surely there are signs in this for the learned.

22.author: Wahiduddin Khan
22.text: Another of His signs is that He created the heavens and earth, and the diversity of your languages and colours. There truly are signs in this for those who know.

22.author: Yusuf Ali
22.text: And among His Signs is the creation of the heavens and the earth, and the variations in your languages and your colours: verily in that are Signs for those who know.

22.author: Transliteration
22.text: Wamin ayatihi khalqu alssamawati waalardi waikhtilafu alsinatikum waalwanikum inna fee thalika laayatin lilAAalimeena

23.author: Ahmed Ali
23.text: Another of His signs is the night, a time for you to sleep, and the day to seek His bounty. Verily there are signs in this for those who pay heed.

23.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
23.text: And among His signs is your sleeping during the night and day, and your seeking His munificence; indeed in this are signs for people who heed.

23.author: Arberry
23.text: And of His signs is your slumbering by night and day, and your seeking after His bounty. Surely in that are signs for a people who hear.

23.author: Daryabadi
23.text: And of His signs are your sleeping by night and by day, and your seeking of His grace verily herein are signs for a people who hearken.

23.author: Hilali & Khan
23.text: And among His Signs is the sleep that you take by night and by day, and your seeking of His Bounty. Verily, in that are indeed signs for a people who listen.

23.author: Itani
23.text: And of His signs are your sleep by night and day, and your pursuit of His bounty. In this are signs for people who listen.

23.author: Maududi
23.text: And of His Signs is your sleeping at night and your seeking His Bounty during the day. Indeed there are Signs in this for those who hearken.

23.author: Mubarakpuri
23.text: And among His signs is your sleep by night and by day, and your seeking His bounty. Verily, in that are indeed signs for a people who listen.

23.author: Pickthall
23.text: And of His signs is your slumber by night and by day, and your seeking of His bounty. Lo! herein indeed are portents for folk who heed.

23.author: Qarai
23.text: And of His signs is your sleep by night and day, and your pursuit of His bounty. There are indeed signs in that for a people who listen.

23.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
23.text: And of His signs is that you sleep at night and day, and seek His bounty. Surely, there are signs in this for those who hear.

23.author: Saheeh International
23.text: And of His signs is your sleep by night and day and your seeking of His bounty. Indeed in that are signs for a people who listen.

23.author: Sarwar
23.text: Your sleeping during the night and in the day your seeking His favors are evidence (of the truth) for the people who have hearing.

23.author: Shakir
23.text: And one of His signs is your sleeping and your seeking of His grace by night and (by) day; most surely there are signs in this for a people who would hear.

23.author: Wahiduddin Khan
23.text: Among His signs are your sleep, at night or in daytime, and your seeking His bounty. There truly are signs in this for people who hear.

23.author: Yusuf Ali
23.text: And among His Signs is the sleep that ye take by night and by day, and the quest that ye (make for livelihood) out of His Bounty: verily in that are signs for those who hearken.

23.author: Transliteration
23.text: Wamin ayatihi manamukum biallayli waalnnahari waibtighaokum min fadlihi inna fee thalika laayatin liqawmin yasmaAAoona

24.author: Ahmed Ali
24.text: Another of His signs is the lightning He shows to fill you both with dread and hope, and the water He sends down from the sky which reawakens the earth that was dead. There are indeed signs in this for those who have sense.

24.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
24.text: And among His signs is that He shows you the lightning, instilling fear and hope, and sends down water from the sky, so revives the earth after its death; indeed in this are signs for people of intellect.

24.author: Arberry
24.text: And of His signs He shows you lightning, for fear and hope, and that He sends down out of heaven water and He revives the earth after it is dead. Surely in that are signs for a people who understand.

24.author: Daryabadi
24.text: And of His signs is that he sheweth you the lightning for a fear's and for a hope, and sendeth down from the heaven water, and therewith quickeneth the earth after the death thereof; verily herein are signs for a people who reflect.

24.author: Hilali & Khan
24.text: And among His Signs is that He shows you the lightning, by way of fear and hope, and He sends down water (rain) from the sky, and therewith revives the earth after its death. Verily, in that are indeed signs for a people who understand.

24.author: Itani
24.text: And of His signs is that He shows you the lightning, causing fear and hope. And He brings down water from the sky, and with it He revives the earth after it was dead. In this are signs for people who understand.

24.author: Maududi
24.text: And of His Signs is that He shows you lightning, arousing both fear and hope, and sends down water from the sky and revives the earth after it is dead. Indeed there are Signs in this for those who use their reason.

24.author: Mubarakpuri
24.text: And among His signs is that He shows you the lightning, for fear and for hope, and He sends down water from the sky, and therewith revives the earth after its death. Verily, in that are indeed signs for a people who understand.

24.author: Pickthall
24.text: And of His signs is this: He showeth you the lightning for a fear and for a hope, and sendeth down water from the sky, and thereby quickeneth the earth after her death. Lo! herein indeed are portents for folk who understand.

24.author: Qarai
24.text: And of His signs is that He shows you the lightning, arousing fear and hope, and He sends down water from the sky, and with it revives the earth after its death. There are indeed signs in that for people who exercise their reason.

24.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
24.text: And of His signs is that He shows you the lightning for fear and hope. He sends down water from the sky and with it He revives the earth after its death. Surely, in this there are signs for a nation that understand.

24.author: Saheeh International
24.text: And of His signs is [that] He shows you the lightning [causing] fear and aspiration, and He sends down rain from the sky by which He brings to life the earth after its lifelessness. Indeed in that are signs for a people who use reason.

24.author: Sarwar
24.text: Also, of the evidence of His existence are His showing you lightening which gives you fear and hope and His sending water down from the sky which revives the earth after its death. In this there is evidence (of the truth) for the people of understanding.

24.author: Shakir
24.text: And one of His signs is that He shows you the lightning for fear and for hope, and sends down water from the clouds then gives life therewith to the earth after its death; most surely there are signs in this for a people who understand

24.author: Wahiduddin Khan
24.text: Among His signs is this: He shows you the lightning, giving rise to [both] fear and hope, and sends down water from the sky, giving life thereby to the earth after it had been lifeless: in this, behold, there are signs indeed for people who use their reason!

24.author: Yusuf Ali
24.text: And among His Signs, He shows you the lightning, by way both of fear and of hope, and He sends down rain from the sky and with it gives life to the earth after it is dead: verily in that are Signs for those who are wise.

24.author: Transliteration
24.text: Wamin ayatihi yureekumu albarqa khawfan watamaAAan wayunazzilu mina alssamai maan fayuhyee bihi alarda baAAda mawtiha inna fee thalika laayatin liqawmin yaAAqiloona

25.author: Ahmed Ali
25.text: Another of His signs is that the heavens and the earth stay in position by His command; and then when He will call you once you will come out of the earth.

25.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
25.text: And among His signs is that the heavens and the earth remain established by His command; then when He calls you – from the earth – you will thereupon emerge.

25.author: Arberry
25.text: And of His signs is that the heaven and earth stand firm by His command; then, when He calls you once and suddenly, out of the earth, lo you shall come forth.

25.author: Daryabadi
25.text: And of His signs is that the heaven and the earth stand fast by His command, and thereafter, when He calleth you, a call from the earth, lo! ye shall come forth.

25.author: Hilali & Khan
25.text: And among His Signs is that the heaven and the earth stand by His Command, then afterwards when He will call you by single call, behold, you will come out from the earth (i.e. from your graves for reckoning and recompense).

25.author: Itani
25.text: And of His signs is that the heaven and the earth stand at His disposal. And then, when He calls you out of the earth, you will emerge at once.

25.author: Maududi
25.text: And of His Signs is that the sky and the earth stand firm by His command. Then no sooner than He summons you out of the earth you will come forth.

25.author: Mubarakpuri
25.text: And among His signs is that the heaven and the earth stand by His command. Then afterwards when He will call you by a single call, behold, you will come out from the earth.

25.author: Pickthall
25.text: And of His signs is this: The heavens and the earth stand fast by His command, and afterward, when He calleth you, lo! from the earth ye will emerge.

25.author: Qarai
25.text: And of His signs is that the sky and the earth stand by His command, and then, when He calls you forth from the earth, behold, you will come forth.

25.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
25.text: And of His signs is that the heaven and earth stand firm at His command. And when He calls you once out of the earth, you shall come forth.

25.author: Saheeh International
25.text: And of His signs is that the heaven and earth remain by His command. Then when He calls you with a [single] call from the earth, immediately you will come forth.

25.author: Sarwar
25.text: Some other evidence of His existence is that both the heavens and the earth stand firm at His command. When He will call you from the earth, you will start to come out.

25.author: Shakir
25.text: And one of His signs is that the heaven and the earth subsist by His command, then when He calls you with a (single) call from out of the earth, lo! you come forth.

25.author: Wahiduddin Khan
25.text: Another of His signs is this: the heavens and the earth stand firm by His command and afterwards when He calls you, behold, from the earth you will come forth.

25.author: Yusuf Ali
25.text: And among His Signs is this, that heaven and earth stand by His Command: then when He calls you, by a single call, from the earth, behold, ye (straightway) come forth.

25.author: Transliteration
25.text: Wamin ayatihi an taqooma alssamao waalardu biamrihi thumma itha daAAakum daAAwatan mina alardi itha antum takhrujoona

26.author: Ahmed Ali
26.text: All those who are in the heavens and the earth are His, and they are all obedient to Him.

26.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
26.text: And to Him only belong all those who are in the heavens and the earth; all are submissive to Him.

26.author: Arberry
26.text: To Him belongs whosoever is in the heavens and the earth; all obey His will.

26.author: Daryabadi
26.text: His is whosoever is in the heavens and the earth; all are unto Him obedient.

26.author: Hilali & Khan
26.text: To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and the earth. All are obedient to Him.

26.author: Itani
26.text: To Him belongs everyone in the heavens and the earth. All are submissive to Him.

26.author: Maududi
26.text: To Him belong all who are in the heavens and all who are on the earth. All are in obedience to Him.

26.author: Mubarakpuri
26.text: To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and the earth. All are obedient to Him.

26.author: Pickthall
26.text: Unto Him belongeth whosoever is in the heavens and the earth. All are obedient unto Him.

26.author: Qarai
26.text: To Him belongs whoever is in the heavens and the earth. All are obedient to Him.

26.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
26.text: To Him belongs whosoever is in the heavens and the earth. All are obedient to His Will.

26.author: Saheeh International
26.text: And to Him belongs whoever is in the heavens and earth. All are to Him devoutly obedient.

26.author: Sarwar
26.text: Everyone in the heavens and the earth belongs to Him and is subservient to Him.

26.author: Shakir
26.text: And His is whosoever is in the heavens and the earth; all are obedient to Him.

26.author: Wahiduddin Khan
26.text: All those in the heavens and on the earth belong to Him. All are obedient to Him.

26.author: Yusuf Ali
26.text: To Him belongs every being that is in the heavens and on earth: all are devoutly obedient to Him.

26.author: Transliteration
26.text: Walahu man fee alssamawati waalardi kullun lahu qanitoona

27.author: Ahmed Ali
27.text: It is He who first creates and then reverts it. This is how His law works inevitably. His semblance is of the most sublime in the heavens and the earth. He is all-mighty and all-wise.

27.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
27.text: And it is He Who initiates the creation, and will then create it again – and this, according to you, should be easier for Him! And for Him only is the Supreme Majesty in the heavens and the earth; and only He is the Almighty, the Wise.

27.author: Arberry
27.text: And it is He who originates creation, then brings it back again, and it is very easy for Him. His is the loftiest likeness in the heavens and the earth; He is the All-mighty, the All-wise.

27.author: Daryabadi
27.text: And He it is who originateth the creation, then shall restore it, and it is easier for Him. His is the most exalted similitude in the heavens and in the earth; and He is the Mighty, the Wise.

27.author: Hilali & Khan
27.text: And He it is Who originates the creation, then will repeat it (after it has been perished), and this is easier for Him. His is the highest description (i.e. none has the right to be worshipped but He, and there is nothing comparable unto Him) in the heavens and in the earth. And He is the All-Mighty, the All-Wise.

27.author: Itani
27.text: It is He who initiates creation, and then repeats it, something easy for Him. His is the highest attribute, in the heavens and the earth. He is the Almighty, the Wise.

27.author: Maududi
27.text: It is He Who creates in the first instance and it is He Who will repeat the creation, and that is easier for Him. His is the loftiest attribute in the heavens and the earth. He is the Most Mighty, the Most Wise.

27.author: Mubarakpuri
27.text: And He it is Who originates the creation, then He will repeat it; and this is easier for Him. His is the highest description in the heavens and in the earth. And He is the All-Mighty, the All-Wise.) Allah says:

27.author: Pickthall
27.text: He it is Who produceth creation, then reproduceth it, and it is easier for Him. His is the Sublime Similitude in the heavens and the earth. He is the Mighty, the Wise.

27.author: Qarai
27.text: It is He who originates the creation, and then He will bring it back—and that is more simple for Him. His is the loftiest description in the heavens and the earth, and He is the All-mighty, the All-wise.

27.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
27.text: It is He who originates the creation, and then brings it back again, that is easier for Him. For Him is the Highest Example in the heavens and earth He is the Almighty, the Wise.

27.author: Saheeh International
27.text: And it is He who begins creation; then He repeats it, and that is [even] easier for Him. To Him belongs the highest attribute in the heavens and earth. And He is the Exalted in Might, the Wise.

27.author: Sarwar
27.text: It is He who begins the creation, then, turns it back. For Him this is very easy. All the exalted attributes in the heavens and the earth belong to Him. He is the Majestic and All-wise.

27.author: Shakir
27.text: And He it is Who originates the creation, then reproduces it, and it is easy to Him; and His are the most exalted attributes in the heavens and the earth, and He is the Mighty, the Wise.

27.author: Wahiduddin Khan
27.text: He is the One who originates creation, then repeats it, and it is very easy for Him. His is the most exalted state in the heavens and on the earth; He is the Mighty, the Wise One.

27.author: Yusuf Ali
27.text: It is He Who begins (the process of) creation; then repeats it; and for Him it is most easy. To Him belongs the loftiest similitude (we can think of) in the heavens and the earth: for He is Exalted in Might, full of wisdom.

27.author: Transliteration
27.text: Wahuwa allathee yabdao alkhalqa thumma yuAAeeduhu wahuwa ahwanu AAalayhi walahu almathalu alaAAla fee alssamawati waalardi wahuwa alAAazeezu alhakeemu

28.author: Ahmed Ali
28.text: He gives you an example from your own life: Do you possess among your dependants any partners in what We have bestowed on you, so that you and they have equal (share) in it, and that you fear them as you fear each other? That is how We explain Our signs clearly for those who comprehend.

28.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
28.text: He illustrates an example for you from your own selves; do you have for yourselves, among the bondmen you possess, partners in the sustenance We have bestowed upon you, thereby you become equal in respect of it – you fearing them the way you fear each other? This is how We illustrate detailed signs for the people of intellect.

28.author: Arberry
28.text: He has struck for you a similitude from yourselves; do you have, among that your right hands own, associates in what We have provided for you so that you are equal in regard to it, you fearing them as you fear each other? So We distinguish the signs for a people who understand.

28.author: Daryabadi
28.text: He propoundeth unto you a similitude taken from yourselves. Have you, from among those whom your right hands own, partners in that wherewith We have provided you, so that ye are equal in respect thereof, and ye fear them as ye fear each other? In this wise We detail the signs for a people who reflect.

28.author: Hilali & Khan
28.text: He sets forth for you a parable from your ownselves, - Do you have partners among those whom your right hands possess (i.e. your slaves) to share as equals in the wealth We have bestowed on you? Whom you fear as you fear each other? Thus do We explain the signs in detail to a people who have sense.

28.author: Itani
28.text: He illustrates an example for you, from your own selves: do you make your servants full partners in the wealth We have given you? Do you revere them as you revere one another? We thus explain the revelations for a people who understand.

28.author: Maududi
28.text: He sets forth for you a parable from your own lives. Do you have among your slaves some who share with you the sustenance that We have bestowed on you so that you become equals in it, all being alike, and then you would hold them in fear as you fear each other? Thus do We make plain the Signs for those who use reason.

28.author: Mubarakpuri
28.text: He sets forth for you a parable from yourselves: Do you have partners among those whom your right hands possess to share as equals in the wealth We have bestowed on you, whom you fear as you fear each other Thus do We explain the signs in detail to a people who have sense.

28.author: Pickthall
28.text: He coineth for you a similitude of yourselves. Have ye, from among those whom your right hands possess, partners in the wealth We have bestowed upon you, equal with you in respect thereof, so that ye fear them as ye fear each other (that ye ascribe unto Us partners out of that which We created)? Thus We display the revelations for people who have sense.

28.author: Qarai
28.text: He draws for you an example from yourselves: Do you have among your slaves any partners [who may share] in what We have provided you, so that you are equal in its respect, and you revere them as you revere one another? Thus do We elaborate the signs for people who exercise their reason.

28.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
28.text: He sets for you an example, drawn from yourselves. Do you have from among those whom your right hand possesses associates in what We have given you, who share it equally with you? Do you fear them as you fear one another? So We made plain Our signs to a nation of understanding.

28.author: Saheeh International
28.text: He presents to you an example from yourselves. Do you have among those whom your right hands possess any partners in what We have provided for you so that you are equal therein [and] would fear them as your fear of one another [within a partnership]? Thus do We detail the verses for a people who use reason.

28.author: Sarwar
28.text: God has told you this parable about yourselves: Could your slaves share your wealth equally with you and could you fear them as you fear yourselves? Thus, do We clarify the evidence (of the truth) for the people of understanding.

28.author: Shakir
28.text: He sets forth to you a parable relating to yourselves: Have you among those whom your right hands possess partners in what We have given you for sustenance, so that with respect to it you are alike; you fear them as you fear each other? Thus do We make the communications distinct for a people who understand.

28.author: Wahiduddin Khan
28.text: He sets forth for you an example taken from your own lives. Do you make your servants full partners with an equal share in the wealth We have bestowed upon you? Do you fear them as you fear each other? In this way We explain the signs to people who use their reason.

28.author: Yusuf Ali
28.text: He does propound to you a similitude from your own (experience): do ye have partners among those whom your right hands possess, to share as equals in the wealth We have bestowed on you? Do ye fear them as ye fear each other? Thus do we explain the Signs in detail to a people that understand.

28.author: Transliteration
28.text: Daraba lakum mathalan min anfusikum hal lakum mi mma malakat aymanukum min shurakaa fee ma razaqnakum faantum feehi sawaon takhafoonahum kakheefatikum anfusakum kathalika nufassilu alayati liqawmin yaAAqiloona

29.author: Ahmed Ali
29.text: And yet the wicked follow their own lusts without understanding. Who can show the way to those whom God allows to go astray? None will they have to help them.

29.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
29.text: Rather the unjust followed their own desires, without knowledge; so who can guide one whom Allah has sent astray? And they do not have supporters.

29.author: Arberry
29.text: Nay, but the evildoers follow their own caprices, without knowledge; so who shall guide those whom God has led astray? They have no helpers.

29.author: Daryabadi
29.text: Aye! those who do wrong follow their own lusts without knowledge. Who, then, will guide him whom Allah hath sent astray? And for them there will be no helpers.

29.author: Hilali & Khan
29.text: Nay, but those who do wrong follow their own lusts without knowledge, Then who will guide him whom Allah has sent astray? And for such there will be no helpers.

29.author: Itani
29.text: Yet the wrongdoers follow their desires without knowledge. But who can guide whom God leaves astray? They will have no helpers.

29.author: Maududi
29.text: But the wrong-doers follow their desires without any knowledge. Who, then, can show the way to him whom Allah lets go astray? Such shall have no helpers.

29.author: Mubarakpuri
29.text: Nay, but those who do wrong follow their own lusts without knowledge. Then who will guide him whom Allah has sent astray And for such there will be no helpers.

29.author: Pickthall
29.text: Nay, but those who do wrong follow their own lusts without knowledge. Who is able to guide him whom Allah hath sent astray? For such there are no helpers.

29.author: Qarai
29.text: Indeed, the wrongdoers follow their own desires without any knowledge. So who will guide those whom Allah has led astray? They will have no helpers.

29.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
29.text: No, the wrongdoers follow their own desires without knowledge. And who can guide those whom Allah has led astray? There shall be none to help them.

29.author: Saheeh International
29.text: But those who wrong follow their [own] desires without knowledge. Then who can guide one whom Allah has sent astray? And for them there are no helpers.

29.author: Sarwar
29.text: In fact, the unjust have followed their desires without knowledge. Who will guide those whom God has caused to go astray? No one will be their helper.

29.author: Shakir
