29.text: Nay! those who are unjust follow their low desires without any knowledge; so who can guide him whom Allah makes err? And they shall have no helpers.

29.author: Wahiduddin Khan
29.text: And still those who are unjust follow their own desires without having any knowledge. Then who can guide those whom God has let go astray? There shall be none to help them.

29.author: Yusuf Ali
29.text: Nay, the wrong-doers (merely) follow their own lusts, being devoid of knowledge. But who will guide those whom Allah leaves astray? To them there will be no helpers.

29.author: Transliteration
29.text: Bali ittabaAAa allatheena thalamoo ahwaahum bighayri AAilmin faman yahdee man adalla Allahu wama lahum min nasireena

30.author: Ahmed Ali
30.text: So keep yourself exclusively on the true way, the creational law of God according to which He created man with the quality of choosing right or wrong. There is no altering of God's creation. This is the supreme law. But most men do not understand.

30.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
30.text: Therefore set your attention for obeying Allah, devoted solely to Him; the foundation set by Allah, upon which He created man; do not change what Allah has created; this is the proper religion – but most people do not know.

30.author: Arberry
30.text: So set thy face to the religion, a man of pure faith -- God's original upon which He originated mankind. There is no changing God's creation. That is the right religion; but most men know it not --

30.author: Daryabadi
30.text: Wherefore set thou thy face towards the true religion uprightly. And follow thou the constitution of Allah according to which He hath constituted mankind. No altering let there be in Allah's creation. That is the right religion, but most men know not.

30.author: Hilali & Khan
30.text: So set you (O Muhammad SAW) your face towards the religion of pure Islamic Monotheism Hanifa (worship none but Allah Alone) Allah's Fitrah (i.e. Allah's Islamic Monotheism), with which He has created mankind. No change let there be in Khalq-illah (i.e. the Religion of Allah Islamic Monotheism), that is the straight religion, but most of men know not. [Tafsir At-Tabari, Vol 21, Page 41]

30.author: Itani
30.text: So devote yourself to the religion of monotheism—the natural instinct God has instilled in mankind. There is no altering God’s creation. This is the true religion, but most people do not know.

30.author: Maududi
30.text: (O Prophet and his followers), turn your face singlemindedly to the true Faith and adhere to the true nature on which Allah has created human beings. The mould fashioned by Allah cannot be altered. That is the True, Straight Faith, although most people do not know.

30.author: Mubarakpuri
30.text: So, set you your face towards the religion as a Hanif. Allah's Fitrah with which He has created mankind. No change let there be in Allah's Khalq, that is the straight religion, but most men know not.

30.author: Pickthall
30.text: So set thy purpose (O Muhammad) for religion as a man by nature upright - the nature (framed) of Allah, in which He hath created man. There is no altering (the laws of) Allah's creation. That is the right religion, but most men know not -

30.author: Qarai
30.text: So set your heart as a person of pure faith on this religion, the original nature endowed by Allah according to which He originated mankind (There is no altering Allah’s creation; that is the upright religion, but most people do not know.)

30.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
30.text: Therefore set your face to the religion purely, the upright creation upon which He originated people. There is no changing of the creation of Allah. This is the valuable religion, although most people do not know?

30.author: Saheeh International
30.text: So direct your face toward the religion, inclining to truth. [Adhere to] the fitrah of Allah upon which He has created [all] people. No change should there be in the creation of Allah. That is the correct religion, but most of the people do not know.

30.author: Sarwar
30.text: (Muhammad), be devoted to the upright religion. It is harmonious with the nature which God has designed for people. The design of God cannot be altered. Thus is the upright religion, but many people do not know.

30.author: Shakir
30.text: Then set your face upright for religion in the right state-- the nature made by Allah in which He has made men; there is no altering of Allah's creation; that is the right religion, but most people do not know--

30.author: Wahiduddin Khan
30.text: Devote yourself single-mindedly to the Religion. And follow the nature [constitution] as made by God, that nature in which He has created mankind. There is no altering the creation of God. That is the right religion. But most people do not realize it.

30.author: Yusuf Ali
30.text: So set thou thy face steadily and truly to the Faith: (establish) Allah's handiwork according to the pattern on which He has made mankind: no change (let there be) in the work (wrought) by Allah: that is the standard Religion: but most among mankind understand not.

30.author: Transliteration
30.text: Faaqim wajhaka lilddeeni haneefan fitrata Allahi allatee fatara alnnasa AAalayha la tabdeela likhalqi Allahi thalika alddeenu alqayyimu walakinna akthara alnnasi la yaAAlamoona

31.author: Ahmed Ali
31.text: Turn towards Him and be dutiful to Him; be firm in devotion, and do not become an idolater,

31.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
31.text: Inclining towards Him – and fear Him, and keep the prayer established, and never be of the polytheists.

31.author: Arberry
31.text: turning to Him. And fear you Him, and perform the prayer, and be not of the idolaters,

31.author: Daryabadi
31.text: And remain turning penitently unto Him, and fear Him, and establish prayer, and be not of the associaters:

31.author: Hilali & Khan
31.text: (Always) Turning in repentance to Him (only), and be afraid and dutiful to Him; and perform As-Salat (Iqamat-as-Salat) and be not of Al-Mushrikun (the disbelievers in the Oneness of Allah, polytheists, idolaters, etc.).

31.author: Itani
31.text: Turning towards Him—and be conscious of Him, and perform the prayer, and do not be of the idolaters.

31.author: Maududi
31.text: (Adhere to the True Faith and) turn to Him, and hold Him in awe, and establish Prayer, and do not be of those who associate others with Allah in His Divinity,

31.author: Mubarakpuri
31.text: (And remain always) turning in repentance to Him and have Taqwa of Him; and perform the Salah and be not of idolators.

31.author: Pickthall
31.text: Turning unto Him (only); and be careful of your duty unto Him and establish worship, and be not of those who ascribe partners (unto Him);

31.author: Qarai
31.text: —turning to Him in penitence, and be wary of Him, and maintain the prayer, and do not be one of the polytheists

31.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
31.text: turning to Him. And fear Him, establish the prayer and do not be of the idolaters,

31.author: Saheeh International
31.text: [Adhere to it], turning in repentance to Him, and fear Him and establish prayer and do not be of those who associate others with Allah

31.author: Sarwar
31.text: Turn in repentance to Him. Have fear of Him. Be steadfast in your prayer. Do not be like the pagans

31.author: Shakir
31.text: Turning to Him, and be careful of (your duty to) Him and keep up prayer and be not of the polytheists

31.author: Wahiduddin Khan
31.text: Turn to Him and fear Him, and be steadfast in prayer, and do not be one of those who associate partners with God,

31.author: Yusuf Ali
31.text: Turn ye back in repentance to Him, and fear Him: establish regular prayers, and be not ye among those who join gods with Allah,-

31.author: Transliteration
31.text: Muneebeena ilayhi waittaqoohu waaqeemoo alssalata wala takoonoo mina almushrikeena

32.author: Ahmed Ali
32.text: (Or) one of those who created rifts in their order and are divided into sects, with each group exulting in what it has (carved out for itself) --

32.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
32.text: Those who broke their religion into several parts and became different groups; every group is happy with what it has.

32.author: Arberry
32.text: even of those who have divided up their religion, and become sects, each several party rejoicing in what is theirs.

32.author: Daryabadi
32.text: Of those who split up their religion and became sects, each band in that which is with them exulting.

32.author: Hilali & Khan
32.text: Of those who split up their religion (i.e. who left the true Islamic Monotheism), and became sects, [i.e. they invented new things in the religion (Bid'ah), and followed their vain desires], each sect rejoicing in that which is with it.

32.author: Itani
32.text: Of those who divided their religion, and became sects; each faction pleased with what they have.

32.author: Maududi
32.text: those who have split up their religion and have become divided into sects, each party exulting in what they have.

32.author: Mubarakpuri
32.text: Of those who split up their religion, and became sects, each sect rejoicing in that which is with it.

32.author: Pickthall
32.text: Of those who split up their religion and became schismatics, each sect exulting in its tenets.

32.author: Qarai
32.text: —those who split up their religion and became sects: each faction boasting about what it possessed.

32.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
32.text: those who have divided their religion, and become sects, each rejoicing in what they have.

32.author: Saheeh International
32.text: [Or] of those who have divided their religion and become sects, every faction rejoicing in what it has.

32.author: Sarwar
32.text: who have divided themselves into various religious sects, each one happy with their own belief.

32.author: Shakir
32.text: Of those who divided their religion and became seas every sect rejoicing in what they had with them

32.author: Wahiduddin Khan
32.text: those who split up their religion and became divided into sects; each one exulting in what they have.

32.author: Yusuf Ali
32.text: Those who split up their Religion, and become (mere) Sects,- each party rejoicing in that which is with itself!

32.author: Transliteration
32.text: Mina allatheena farraqoo deenahum wakanoo shiyaAAan kullu hizbin bima ladayhim farihoona

33.author: Ahmed Ali
33.text: When misfortune befalls men they pray to their Lord and turn to Him; but afterwards when He has given them a taste of His benevolence a section of them begins to ascribe compeers to their Lord

33.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
33.text: And when some affliction reaches men, they pray to their Lord inclining towards Him – and when He gives them a taste of His mercy, thereupon a group among them begins setting up partners to their Lord!

33.author: Arberry
33.text: When some affliction visits mankind, they call unto their Lord, turning to Him; then, when He lets them taste mercy from Him, lo, a party of them assign associates to their Lord,

33.author: Daryabadi
33.text: And when hurt toucheth mankind they cry unto their Lord, turning penitently unto Him; then when He causeth them to taste of His mercy, lo! a part of them with their Lord associate others.

33.author: Hilali & Khan
33.text: And when harm touches men, they cry sincerely only to their Lord (Allah), turning to Him in repentance, but when He gives them a taste of His Mercy, behold! a party of them associate partners in worship with their Lord.

33.author: Itani
33.text: When affliction touches the people, they call on their Lord, turning to Him in repentance. But then, when He gives them a taste of His mercy, some of them attribute partners to their Lord.

33.author: Maududi
33.text: (Such are human beings) that when any misfortune befalls them, they cry to their Lord, penitently turning to Him. But no sooner that He lets them have a taste of His Mercy than some of them begin associating others with their Lord in His Divinity

33.author: Mubarakpuri
33.text: And when harm touches men, they cry sincerely only to their Lord, turning to Him in repentance; but when He gives them a taste of His mercy, behold, a party of them associates partners in worship with their Lord.

33.author: Pickthall
33.text: And when harm toucheth men they cry unto their Lord, turning to Him in repentance; then, when they have tasted of His mercy, behold! some of them attribute partners to their Lord

33.author: Qarai
33.text: When distress befalls people, they supplicate their Lord, turning to Him in penitence. Then, when He lets them taste His mercy, behold, a part of them ascribe partners to their Lord,

33.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
33.text: When affliction befalls mankind they turn to Him calling their Lord in prayer, but when He lets them taste His Mercy, some of them assign associates to their Lord,

33.author: Saheeh International
33.text: And when adversity touches the people, they call upon their Lord, turning in repentance to Him. Then when He lets them taste mercy from Him, at once a party of them associate others with their Lord,

33.author: Sarwar
33.text: When people face hardship, they begin praying to their Lord and turn in repentance to Him. When they receive mercy from Him, a group of them begin to consider things equal to God,

33.author: Shakir
33.text: And when harm afflicts men, they call upon their Lord, turning to Him, then when He makes them taste of mercy from Him, lo! some of them begin to associate (others) with their Lord,

33.author: Wahiduddin Khan
33.text: When an affliction befalls men, they cry out to their Lord, turning to Him in repentance; but then, when He has made them taste His mercy, a section of them associate partners with their Lord,

33.author: Yusuf Ali
33.text: When trouble touches men, they cry to their Lord, turning back to Him in repentance: but when He gives them a taste of Mercy as from Himself, behold, some of them pay part-worship to other god's besides their Lord,-

33.author: Transliteration
33.text: Waitha massa alnnasa durrun daAAaw rabbahum muneebeena ilayhi thumma itha athaqahum minhu rahmatan itha fareequn minhum birabbihim yushrikoona

34.author: Ahmed Ali
34.text: So as to deny what We have given them. Please yourselves for a time; you will come to know soon.

34.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
34.text: In order to be ungrateful for Our bestowal; “Therefore enjoy”; you will soon come to know.

34.author: Arberry
34.text: that they may be ungrateful for what We have given them. 'Take your enjoyment; certainly you will soon know.'

34.author: Daryabadi
34.text: So that they may be ungrateful for that which We have vouchsafed to them. So enjoy awhile; presently ye shall come to know.

34.author: Hilali & Khan
34.text: So as to be ungrateful for the graces which We have bestowed on them. Then enjoy (your short life); but you will come to know.

34.author: Itani
34.text: To show ingratitude for what We have given them. Indulge yourselves—you will surely know.

34.author: Maududi
34.text: so that they may show ingratitude to Us for the favours We had bestowed upon them. So, enjoy yourselves a while; but then you shall soon come to know.

34.author: Mubarakpuri
34.text: So as to be ungrateful for the graces which We have bestowed on them. Then enjoy; but you will come to know.

34.author: Pickthall
34.text: So as to disbelieve in that which We have given them. (Unto such it is said): Enjoy yourselves awhile, but ye will come to know.

34.author: Qarai
34.text: being ungrateful toward what We have given them. So let them enjoy. Soon they will know!

34.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
34.text: disbelieving in what We have given them. Enjoy, but you shall soon know.

34.author: Saheeh International
34.text: So that they will deny what We have granted them. Then enjoy yourselves, for you are going to know.

34.author: Sarwar
34.text: because of their ingratitude for what We have given them. Let them enjoy themselves, but they will soon know (the consequences of their deeds).

34.author: Shakir
34.text: So as to be ungrateful for what We have given them; but enjoy yourselves (for a while), for you shall soon come to know.

34.author: Wahiduddin Khan
34.text: and are ungrateful for what We have given them. So enjoy yourselves for a while, but soon you will come to know.

34.author: Yusuf Ali
34.text: (As if) to show their ingratitude for the (favours) We have bestowed on them! Then enjoy (your brief day); but soon will ye know (your folly).

34.author: Transliteration
34.text: Liyakfuroo bima ataynahum fatamattaAAoo fasawfa taAAlamoona

35.author: Ahmed Ali
35.text: Have We sent down a charter to them which mentions what they associate with Him?

35.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
35.text: Or have We sent down to them any proof, which therefore tells them of My partners?

35.author: Arberry
35.text: Or have We sent down any authority upon them, such as speaks of that they associate with Him?

35.author: Daryabadi
35.text: Or have We sent unto them any authority, so that it speaketh of that which they have been with Him associating?

35.author: Hilali & Khan
35.text: Or have We revealed to them a Scripture, which speaks of that which they have been associating with Him?

35.author: Itani
35.text: Have We sent down to them any authority, which speaks in support of their idols?

35.author: Maududi
35.text: Have We sent down any sanction which provides support to their associating others with Allah in His Divinity?

35.author: Mubarakpuri
35.text: Or have We revealed to them an authority, which speaks of that which they have been associating with Him

35.author: Pickthall
35.text: Or have We revealed unto them any warrant which speaketh of that which they associate with Him?

35.author: Qarai
35.text: Have We sent down to them any authority which might assert what they associate with Him?

35.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
35.text: Or have We sent down to them an authority that speaks of that they associate with Him?

35.author: Saheeh International
35.text: Or have We sent down to them an authority, and it speaks of what they were associating with Him?

35.author: Sarwar
35.text: Have We sent them any authority to speak in support of their idols?

35.author: Shakir
35.text: Or, have We sent down upon them an authority so that it speaks of that which they associate with Him?

35.author: Wahiduddin Khan
35.text: Have We sent down to them any authority which speaks in favour of what they associate with Him?

35.author: Yusuf Ali
35.text: Or have We sent down authority to them, which points out to them the things to which they pay part-worship?

35.author: Transliteration
35.text: Am anzalna AAalayhim sultanan fahuwa yatakallamu bima kanoo bihi yushrikoona

36.author: Ahmed Ali
36.text: When We give men a taste of Our benevolence they start rejoicing in it. When misfortune befalls them as a result of what they have done themselves, they begin to despair.

36.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
36.text: And when We give people the taste of mercy they rejoice at it; and if an ill fortune reaches them because of what their hands have sent ahead – thereupon they lose hope!

36.author: Arberry
36.text: And when We let men taste mercy, they rejoice in it; but if some evil befalls them for that their own hands have forwarded, behold, they despair.

36.author: Daryabadi
36.text: And when We cause mankind to taste of mercy they exult thereat; then if an evil befalleth them because of that which their hands have sent forth, lo! they despair.

36.author: Hilali & Khan
36.text: And when We cause mankind to taste of mercy, they rejoice therein, but when some evil afflicts them because of (evil deeds and sins) that their (own) hands have sent forth, lo! They are in despair!

36.author: Itani
36.text: When We give people a taste of mercy, they rejoice in it. But when adversity befalls them, because of what their hands have perpetrated, they begin to despair.

36.author: Maududi
36.text: When We make people have a taste of Our Mercy, they exult in it; and when any misfortune befalls them in consequence of their deeds, then lo and behold, they despair.

36.author: Mubarakpuri
36.text: And when We cause mankind to taste of mercy, they rejoice therein; but when some evil afflicts them because of what their hands have sent forth, behold, they are in despair!

36.author: Pickthall
36.text: And when We cause mankind to taste of mercy they rejoice therein; but if an evil thing befall them as the consequence of their own deeds, lo! they are in despair!

36.author: Qarai
36.text: When We let people taste [Our] mercy, they boast about it; but should an ill visit them because of what their hands have sent ahead, behold, they become despondent!

36.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
36.text: When We give people a taste of mercy, they rejoice in it, but when evil befalls them through the forwarding of their own hands, they become despondent.

36.author: Saheeh International
36.text: And when We let the people taste mercy, they rejoice therein, but if evil afflicts them for what their hands have put forth, immediately they despair.

36.author: Sarwar
36.text: When people receive mercy, they are happy with it. However, when hardship befalls them because of their own deeds, they despair.

36.author: Shakir
36.text: And when We make people taste of mercy they rejoice in it, and if an evil befall them for what their hands have already wrought, lo! they are in despair.

36.author: Wahiduddin Khan
36.text: When We give mankind a taste of Our blessing, they rejoice therein: but if they encounter tribulation because of their own actions -- they fall into despair.

36.author: Yusuf Ali
36.text: When We give men a taste of Mercy, they exult thereat: and when some evil afflicts them because of what their (own) hands have sent forth, behold, they are in despair!

36.author: Transliteration
36.text: Waitha athaqna alnnasa rahmatan farihoo biha wain tusibhum sayyiatun bima qaddamat aydeehim itha hum yaqnatoona

37.author: Ahmed Ali
37.text: Do they not see God increases or decreases the means of whosoever He please? Verily there are signs in this for those who believe.

37.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
37.text: And did they not see that Allah eases the sustenance for whomever He wills and restricts it for whomever He wills? Indeed in this are signs for people who believe.

37.author: Arberry
37.text: Have they not seen that God outspreads and straitens His provision to whom He will? Surely in that are signs for a people who believe.

37.author: Daryabadi
37.text: Behold they not that expandeth the provision for whomsoever He and stinteth? Verily herein are signs for a people who believe.

37.author: Hilali & Khan
37.text: Do they not see that Allah enlarges the provision for whom He wills and straitens (it for whom He wills). Verily, in that are indeed signs for a people who believe.

37.author: Itani
37.text: Do they not see that God expands the provision for whomever He wills, or restricts it? Surely in this are signs for people who believe.

37.author: Maududi
37.text: Do they not see that Allah enlarges and straitens the sustenance of those whom He pleases? There are Signs in this for those who believe.

37.author: Mubarakpuri
37.text: Do they not see that Allah expands the provision for whom He wills and straitens (it for whom He wills). Verily, in that are indeed signs for a people who believe.

37.author: Pickthall
37.text: See they not that Allah enlargeth the provision for whom He will, and straiteneth (it for whom He will). Lo! herein indeed are portents for folk who believe.

37.author: Qarai
37.text: Do they not see that Allah expands the provision for whomever He wishes, and tightens it? There are indeed signs in that for a people who have faith.

37.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
37.text: Do they not see that Allah outspreads and restricts His provision to whom He will? Surely, there are signs in this for those who believe.

37.author: Saheeh International
37.text: Do they not see that Allah extends provision for whom He wills and restricts [it]? Indeed, in that are signs for a people who believe.

37.author: Sarwar
37.text: Have they not seen how God increases the livelihood of whomever He wants and determines his share. In this there is evidence (of the truth) for the believing people.

37.author: Shakir
37.text: Do they not see that Allah makes ample provision for whom He pleases, or straitens? Most surely there are signs in this for a people who believe.

37.author: Wahiduddin Khan
37.text: Do they not see that God gives abundantly to whoever He pleases, and sparingly to whoever He pleases? In that truly there are signs for those who believe.

37.author: Yusuf Ali
37.text: See they not that Allah enlarges the provision and restricts it, to whomsoever He pleases? Verily in that are Signs for those who believe.

37.author: Transliteration
37.text: Awalam yaraw anna Allaha yabsutu alrrizqa liman yashao wayaqdiru inna fee thalika laayatin liqawmin yuminoona

38.author: Ahmed Ali
38.text: So, give their share to the relatives, the needy, and the wayfarers. This is best for those who seek the way that leads to God, and they will be successful.

38.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
38.text: Therefore give the relative his right, and to the needy, and to the traveller; this is better for those who seek the pleasure of Allah; and it is they who are the successful.

38.author: Arberry
38.text: And give the kinsman his right, and the needy, and the traveller; that is better for those who desire God's Face; those they are the prosperers.

38.author: Daryabadi
38.text: So give thou unto the kinsman his due and unto the needy and unto the wayfarer. That is best for those who seek Allah's countenance; and those: they are the blissful ones.

38.author: Hilali & Khan
38.text: So give to the kindred his due, and to Al-Miskin (the poor) and to the wayfarer. That is best for those who seek Allah's Countenance, and it is they who will be successful.

38.author: Itani
38.text: So give the relative his rights, and the destitute, and the wayfarer. That is best for those who seek God’s presence. Those are the prosperous.

38.author: Maududi
38.text: So give his due to the near of kin, and to the needy, and to the wayfarer. That is better for those who desire to please Allah. It is they who will prosper.

38.author: Mubarakpuri
38.text: So, give to the kindred his due, and to Al-Miskin and to the wayfarer. That is best for those who seek Allah's Face; and it is they who will be successful.

38.author: Pickthall
38.text: So give to the kinsman his due, and to the needy, and to the wayfarer. That is best for those who seek Allah's Countenance. And such are they who are successful.

38.author: Qarai
38.text: Give the relative his due, and the needy and the traveller [as well]. That is better for those who seek Allah’s pleasure, and it is they who are the felicitous.

38.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
38.text: And give to the kinsman his due, and to the needy, and to the destitute traveler. That is best for those who desire the Face of Allah; such will surely prosper.

38.author: Saheeh International
38.text: So give the relative his right, as well as the needy and the traveler. That is best for those who desire the countenance of Allah, and it is they who will be the successful.

38.author: Sarwar
38.text: Give the relatives, the destitute, and the needy travellers their share (of charity). It is better for those who want to please God and they will have everlasting happiness.

38.author: Shakir
38.text: Then give to the near of kin his due, and to the needy and the wayfarer; this is best for those who desire Allah's pleasure, and these it is who are successful.

38.author: Wahiduddin Khan
38.text: So give the near relative, the needy, and the wayfarer their due -- that is best for those who seek God's pleasure: such men are the ones who will surely prosper.

38.author: Yusuf Ali
38.text: So give what is due to kindred, the needy, and the wayfarer. That is best for those who seek the Countenance, of Allah, and it is they who will prosper.

38.author: Transliteration
38.text: Faati tha alqurba haqqahu waalmiskeena waibna alssabeeli thalika khayrun lillatheena yureedoona wajha Allahi waolaika humu almuflihoona

39.author: Ahmed Ali
39.text: What you give on interest to increase (your capital) through others' wealth, does not find increase with God; yet what you give (in alms and charity) with a pure heart, seeking the way of God, will be doubled.

39.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
39.text: And that which you give upon usury, in order that it may increase the creditors’ property, will not increase before Allah; and the charity you give seeking the pleasure of Allah – only that will increase manifold.

39.author: Arberry
39.text: And what you give in usury, that it may increase upon the people's wealth, increases not with God; but what you give in alms, desiring God's Face, those -- they receive recompense manifold.

39.author: Daryabadi
39.text: And whatsoever ye give in gift in order that it may increase among the substance of men increaseth not with Allah; and whatsoever ye give in poor-rate seeking the countenance of Allah-then those: they shall, have increase manifold.

39.author: Hilali & Khan
39.text: And that which you give in gift (to others), in order that it may increase (your wealth by expecting to get a better one in return) from other people's property, has no increase with Allah, but that which you give in Zakat seeking Allah's Countenance then those, they shall have manifold increase.

39.author: Itani
39.text: The usury you practice, seeking thereby to multiply people’s wealth, will not multiply with God. But what you give in charity, desiring God’s approval—these are the multipliers.

39.author: Maududi
39.text: Whatever you pay as interest so that it may increase the wealth of people does not increase in the sight of Allah. As for the Zakah that you give, seeking with it Allah's good pleasure, that is multiplied manifold.

39.author: Mubarakpuri
39.text: And that which you give in Riba in order that it may increase from other people's property, has no increase with Allah; but that which you give in Zakah seeking Allah's Face, then those they shall have manifold increase.

39.author: Pickthall
39.text: That which ye give in usury in order that it may increase on (other) people's property hath no increase with Allah; but that which ye give in charity, seeking Allah's Countenance, hath increase manifold.

39.author: Qarai
39.text: What you give in usury in order that it may increase people’s wealth does not increase with Allah. But what you pay as zakat seeking Allah’s pleasure—it is they who will be given a manifold increase.

39.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
39.text: That which you give in usury so that it increases in other people's wealth, will not increase with Allah; but the charity you give desiring the Face of Allah, to those, they shall be recompensed many times over.

39.author: Saheeh International
39.text: And whatever you give for interest to increase within the wealth of people will not increase with Allah. But what you give in zakah, desiring the countenance of Allah - those are the multipliers.

39.author: Sarwar
39.text: God will not allow to increase whatever illegal interest you try to receive in order to increase your wealth at the expense of people's property. Whatever amount of zakat you give to please God will be doubled (for you).

39.author: Shakir
39.text: And whatever you lay out as usury, so that it may increase in the property of men, it shall not increase with Allah; and whatever you give in charity, desiring Allah's pleasure-- it is these (persons) that shall get manifold.

39.author: Wahiduddin Khan
39.text: Whatever you lend out in usury to gain in value through other people's wealth will not increase in God's eyes, but whatever you give in alms in your desire for God's pleasure will be multiplied.

39.author: Yusuf Ali
39.text: That which ye lay out for increase through the property of (other) people, will have no increase with Allah: but that which ye lay out for charity, seeking the Countenance of Allah, (will increase): it is these who will get a recompense multiplied.

39.author: Transliteration
39.text: Wama ataytum min riban liyarbuwa fee amwali alnnasi fala yarboo AAinda Allahi wama ataytum min zakatin tureedoona wajha Allahi faolaika humu almudAAifoona

40.author: Ahmed Ali
40.text: It is God who created you, then gave you sustenance, then He will make you die, and bring you back to life. Is there one among those you associate with Him who can do the least of these things? Too high and exalted is He for what they associate with Him!

40.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
40.text: It is Allah Who created you and then provided you sustenance, then will cause you to die, and will then give you life again; is there any among your ascribed partners that can do any of these things? Purity and Supremacy are to Him, above their ascribing of partners (to Him)!

40.author: Arberry
40.text: God is He that created you, then He provided for you, then He shall make you dead, then He shall give you life; is there any of your associates does aught of that? Glory be to Him! High be He exalted above that they associate!

40.author: Daryabadi
40.text: Allah is He Who created you and provided food for you, then He causeth you to die, and then He shall quicken you. Is there any of your associate-gods that doth aught of that? Hallowed and exalted be He above that which they associate!

40.author: Hilali & Khan
40.text: Allah is He Who created you, then provided food for you, then will cause you to die, then (again) He will give you life (on the Day of Resurrection). Is there any of your (so-called) partners (of Allah) that do anything of that? Glory be to Him! And Exalted be He above all that (evil) they associate (with Him).

40.author: Itani
40.text: God is He who created you, then provides for you, then makes you die, then brings you back to life. Can any of your idols do any of that? Glorified is He, and Exalted above what they associate.

40.author: Maududi
40.text: It is Allah Who created you, then bestowed upon you your sustenance, and He will cause you to die and then will bring you back to life. Can any of those whom you associate with Allah in His Divinity! do any such thing? Glory be to Him and exalted be He above whatever they associate with Allah in His Divinity.

40.author: Mubarakpuri
40.text: Allah is He Who created you, then provided food for you, then will cause you to die, then He will give you life. Is there any of your partners that do anything of that Glory be to Him! And Exalted be He above all that they associate.

40.author: Pickthall
40.text: Allah is He Who created you and then sustained you, then causeth you to die, then giveth life to you again. Is there any of your (so-called) partners (of Allah) that doeth aught of that? Praised and Exalted be He above what they associate (with Him)!

40.author: Qarai
40.text: It is Allah who created you and then He provided for you, then He makes you die, then He will bring you to life. Is there anyone among your ‘partners’ who does anything of that kind? Immaculate is He and exalted above [having] any partners that they ascribe [to Him]!

40.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
40.text: It is Allah who has created you and given you your provision. He will cause you to die, and will then revive you. Can any of your associates do any of that? Exalted is He above what they associate.

40.author: Saheeh International
40.text: Allah is the one who created you, then provided for you, then will cause you to die, and then will give you life. Are there any of your "partners" who does anything of that? Exalted is He and high above what they associate with Him.

40.author: Sarwar
40.text: It is God who has created you and given you sustenance. He will make you die and will bring you back to life. Can any of your idols do such things? God is too Exalted to be considered equal to anything else.

40.author: Shakir
40.text: Allah is He Who created you, then gave you sustenance, then He causes you to die, then brings you to life. Is there any of your associate-gods who does aught of it? Glory be to Him, and exalted be He above what they associate (with Him).

40.author: Wahiduddin Khan
40.text: God is He who created you, then provides for you, then will cause you to die and then bring you back to life. Can any of your "partners" do any one of these things? Glory be to Him and exalted be He above anything they associate with Him!

40.author: Yusuf Ali
40.text: It is Allah Who has created you: further, He has provided for your sustenance; then He will cause you to die; and again He will give you life. Are there any of your (false) "Partners" who can do any single one of these things? Glory to Him! and high is He above the partners they attribute (to him)!

40.author: Transliteration
40.text: Allahu allathee khalaqakum thumma razaqakum thumma yumeetukum thumma yuhyeekum hal min shurakaikum man yafAAalu min thalikum min shayin subhanahu wataAAala AAamma yushrikoona

41.author: Ahmed Ali
41.text: Corruption has spread over land and sea from what men have done themselves that they may taste a little of what they have done: They may haply come back (to the right path).

41.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
41.text: Chaos appears in the land and the sea because of the evil deeds which people’s hands have earned, in order to make them taste the flavour of some of their misdeeds – in order that they may come back.

41.author: Arberry
41.text: Corruption has appeared in the land and sea, for that men's own hands have earned, that He may let them taste some part of that which they have done, that haply so they may return.

41.author: Daryabadi
41.text: Corruptness hath appeared on land and sea because of that which men's hands have earned, so that he may make them taste a part of that which they have worked, in order that haply they may turn.

41.author: Hilali & Khan
41.text: Evil (sins and disobedience of Allah, etc.) has appeared on land and sea because of what the hands of men have earned (by oppression and evil deeds, etc.), that Allah may make them taste a part of that which they have done, in order that they may return (by repenting to Allah, and begging His Pardon).

41.author: Itani
41.text: Corruption has appeared on land and sea, because of what people’s hands have earned, in order to make them taste some of what they have done, so that they might return.

41.author: Maududi
41.text: Evil has become rife on the land and at sea because of men's deeds; this in order that He may cause them to have a taste of some of their deeds; perhaps they will turn back (from evil).

41.author: Mubarakpuri
41.text: Evil has appeared in Al-Barr and Al-Bahr because of what the hands of men have earned, that He may make them taste a part of that which they have done, in order that they may return.

41.author: Pickthall
41.text: Corruption doth appear on land and sea because of (the evil) which men's hands have done, that He may make them taste a part of that which they have done, in order that they may return.

41.author: Qarai
41.text: Corruption has appeared in land and sea because of the doings of the people’s hands, that He may make them taste something of what they have done, so that they may come back.

41.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
41.text: Corruption has appeared on land and sea with what the hands of the people earned. Therefore, they taste some of what they did in order that they return.

41.author: Saheeh International
41.text: Corruption has appeared throughout the land and sea by [reason of] what the hands of people have earned so He may let them taste part of [the consequence of] what they have done that perhaps they will return [to righteousness].

41.author: Sarwar
41.text: Evil has spread over the land and the sea because of human deeds and through these God will cause some people to suffer so that perhaps they will return to Him.

41.author: Shakir
41.text: Corruption has appeared in the land and the sea on account of what the hands of men have wrought, that He may make them taste a part of that which they have done, so that they may return.

41.author: Wahiduddin Khan
41.text: Corruption has appeared on land and sea because of the evil which men's hands have done: and so He will make them taste the fruit of some of their doings, so that they may turn back from evil.

41.author: Yusuf Ali
41.text: Mischief has appeared on land and sea because of (the meed) that the hands of men have earned, that (Allah) may give them a taste of some of their deeds: in order that they may turn back (from Evil).

41.author: Transliteration
41.text: Thahara alfasadu fee albarri waalbahri bima kasabat aydee alnnasi liyutheeqahum baAAda allathee AAamiloo laAAallahum yarjiAAoona

42.author: Ahmed Ali
42.text: Say: "Travel on the earth and see how came the end of those before you." Most of them were idolaters.

42.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
42.text: Proclaim (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him), “Travel in the land, and see what sort of fate befell the former people; and most of them were polytheists.”

42.author: Arberry
42.text: Say: 'Journey in the land, then behold how was the end of those that were before; most of them were idolaters.'

42.author: Daryabadi
42.text: Say thou: go forth in the land and behold what wise hath been the end of those of aforetime? And most of them were associaters.

42.author: Hilali & Khan
42.text: Say (O Muhammad SAW): "Travel in the land and see what was the end of those before (you)! Most of them were Mushrikun (the disbelievers in the Oneness of Allah, polytheists, idolaters, etc.)."

42.author: Itani
42.text: Say, “Roam the earth, and observe the fate of those who came before. Most of them were idolaters.”

42.author: Maududi
42.text: (O Prophet), say: “Traverse in the earth and see what was the end of those who went before you: most of them associated others with Allah in His Divinity.”

42.author: Mubarakpuri
42.text: Say: "Travel in the land and see what was the end of those before (you)! Most of them were idolators."

42.author: Pickthall
42.text: Say (O Muhammad, to the disbelievers): Travel in the land, and see the nature of the consequence for those who were before you! Most of them were idolaters.

42.author: Qarai
42.text: Say, ‘Travel over the land, and see how was the fate of those who were before [you], most of whom were polytheists.’

42.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
42.text: Say: 'Journey in the land and see what was the end of those who were before you. Most of them were idolaters'

42.author: Saheeh International
42.text: Say, [O Muhammad], "Travel through the land and observe how was the end of those before. Most of them were associators [of others with Allah].

42.author: Sarwar
42.text: (Muhammad), tell them, "Travel through the land to see how terrible was the end of those who lived before. Many of them were pagans.

42.author: Shakir
42.text: Say: Travel in the land, then see how was the end of those before; most of them were polytheists.

42.author: Wahiduddin Khan
42.text: Say, "Journey through the land, and see how those before you met their end -- most of them, ascribed partners with God."

42.author: Yusuf Ali
42.text: Say: "Travel through the earth and see what was the end of those before (you): Most of them worshipped others besides Allah."

42.author: Transliteration
42.text: Qul seeroo fee alardi faonthuroo kayfa kana AAaqibatu allatheena min qablu kana aktharuhum mushrikeena

43.author: Ahmed Ali
43.text: So set your face towards the straight path before the day arrives from God which is irreversible. Men will be segregated on that day.

43.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
43.text: Therefore set your attention for worshipping, before the day from Allah which cannot be averted – on that day people will be split, separated.

43.author: Arberry
43.text: So set thy face to the true religion before there comes a day from God that cannot be turned back; on that day they shall be sundered apart.

43.author: Daryabadi
43.text: So set thy face toward the right religion before the Day cometh from which there is no averting, from Allah, on that Day they shall be sundered.

43.author: Hilali & Khan
43.text: So set you (O Muhammad SAW) your face to (the obedience of Allah, your Lord) the straight and right religion (Islamic Monotheism), before there comes from Allah a Day which none can avert it. On that Day men shall be divided [(in two groups), a group in Paradise and a group in Hell].

43.author: Itani
43.text: So devote yourself to the upright religion, before there comes from God a Day that cannot be averted. On that Day, they will be shocked.

43.author: Maududi
43.text: So turn your face exclusively towards the True Faith before there comes the Day whose coming from Allah cannot be averted, the Day when people will split into groups.

43.author: Mubarakpuri
43.text: So, set you your face to the straight and right religion, before there comes from Allah a Day which none can avert. On that Day men shall be divided.

43.author: Pickthall
43.text: So set thy purpose resolutely for the right religion, before the inevitable day cometh from Allah. On that day mankind will be sundered-

43.author: Qarai
43.text: So set your heart on the upright religion, before there comes a day irrevocable from Allah. On that day they shall be split [into various groups].

43.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
43.text: Therefore in purity set your face to the religion, before there comes from Allah a Day that cannot be turned back. On that Day mankind will be separated.

43.author: Saheeh International
43.text: So direct your face toward the correct religion before a Day comes from Allah of which there is no repelling. That Day, they will be divided.

43.author: Sarwar
43.text: Be devoted to the upright religion before the coming of the inevitable day when no one can escape from God and people will either be sent to Paradise or hell."

43.author: Shakir
43.text: Then turn thy face straight to the right religion before there come from Allah the day which cannot be averted; on that day they shall become separated.

43.author: Wahiduddin Khan
43.text: [Prophet], set your face to the right religion, before that Day comes from God which cannot be averted. On that Day, mankind will be parted in two.

43.author: Yusuf Ali
43.text: But set thou thy face to the right Religion before there come from Allah the Day which there is no chance of averting: on that Day shall men be divided (in two).

43.author: Transliteration
43.text: Faaqim wajhaka lilddeeni alqayyimi min qabli an yatiya yawmun la maradda lahu mina Allahi yawmaithin yassaddaAAoona

44.author: Ahmed Ali
44.text: So that he who disbelieves will bear the consequence of his unbelief; and he who does the right will straighten out the way for himself,

44.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
44.text: Whoever disbelieves, then the calamity of his disbelief is only on him; and those who do good deeds, are preparing for themselves.

44.author: Arberry
44.text: Whoso disbelieves, his unbelief shall be charged against him; and whosoever does righteousness for themselves they are making provision,

44.author: Daryabadi
44.text: Whosoever disbelieveth, on him is his infidelity, and those who work righteously are preparing for themselves.

44.author: Hilali & Khan
44.text: Whosoever disbelieves will suffer from his disbelief, and whosoever does righteous good deeds (by practising Islamic Monotheism), then such will prepare a good place (in Paradise) for themselves (and will be saved by Allah from His Torment).

44.author: Itani
44.text: Whoever disbelieves, upon him falls his disbelief. And whoever acts righteously—they are preparing for themselves.

44.author: Maududi
44.text: He who disbelieves will suffer the consequence of it and he who acts righteously, they will pave the way for their own good

44.author: Mubarakpuri
44.text: Whosoever disbelieves will suffer from his disbelief, and whosoever does righteous good deeds, then such will prepare a good place for themselves.

44.author: Pickthall
44.text: Whoso disbelieveth must (then) bear the consequences of his disbelief, while those who do right make provision for themselves -

44.author: Qarai
44.text: Whoever is faithless shall face the consequences of his faithlessness, and those who act righteously only prepare for their own souls,

44.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
44.text: Those who disbelieve will be charged for their disbelief, while the righteous are making provision for themselves

44.author: Saheeh International
44.text: Whoever disbelieves - upon him is [the consequence of] his disbelief. And whoever does righteousness - they are for themselves preparing,

44.author: Sarwar
44.text: Those who disbelieve do so against their own souls. Those who do good pave the way for their own benefit.

44.author: Shakir
44.text: Whoever disbelieves, he shall be responsible for his disbelief, and whoever does good, they prepare (good) for their own souls,

44.author: Wahiduddin Khan
44.text: Those who rejected the truth will bear the burden of that rejection, and those who did good deeds will have made good provision for themselves.

44.author: Yusuf Ali
44.text: Those who reject Faith will suffer from that rejection: and those who work righteousness will spread their couch (of repose) for themselves (in heaven):

44.author: Transliteration
44.text: Man kafara faAAalayhi kufruhu waman AAamila salihan falianfusihim yamhadoona

45.author: Ahmed Ali
45.text: So that God may reward those who believed and did what was good, by His grace. Surely He does not love unbelievers.

45.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
45.text: In order to reward those who believed and did good deeds, by His munificence; indeed He does not like the disbelievers.

45.author: Arberry
45.text: that He may recompense those who believe and do righteous deeds of His bounty; He loves not the unbelievers.

45.author: Daryabadi
45.text: So that he shall recompense those who believe and work righteous works out of His grace; verily He loveth not the infidels.

45.author: Hilali & Khan
45.text: That He may reward those who believe (in the Oneness of Allah Islamic Monotheism), and do righteous good deeds, out of His Bounty. Verily, He likes not the disbelievers.

45.author: Itani
45.text: So that He may reward those who have believed and done the righteous deeds out of His bounty. Indeed, He does not love the ungrateful.

45.author: Maududi
45.text: so that Allah may, out of His Bounty, reward those who believe and act righteously. Verily He does not love the unbelievers.

45.author: Mubarakpuri
45.text: That He may reward those who believe and do righteous good deeds, out of His bounty. Verily, He likes not the disbelievers.

45.author: Pickthall
45.text: That He may reward out of His bounty those who believe and do good works. Lo! He loveth not the disbelievers (in His guidance).

45.author: Qarai
45.text: so that He may reward those who have faith and do righteous deeds out of His grace. Indeed, He does not like the faithless.

45.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
45.text: so that He will recompense those who believe and do righteous deeds from His bounty. He does not love the unbelievers.

45.author: Saheeh International
45.text: That He may reward those who have believed and done righteous deeds out of His bounty. Indeed, He does not like the disbelievers.

45.author: Sarwar
45.text: God will reward the righteously striving believers through His favor. He does not love the unbelievers.

45.author: Shakir
45.text: That He may reward those who believe and do good out of His grace; surely He does not love the unbelievers.

45.author: Wahiduddin Khan
45.text: For then He will reward out of His bounty those who believe and do good deeds; He does not love those who reject the truth.

45.author: Yusuf Ali
45.text: That He may reward those who believe and work righteous deeds, out of his Bounty. For He loves not those who reject Faith.

45.author: Transliteration
45.text: Liyajziya allatheena amanoo waAAamiloo alssalihati min fadlihi innahu la yuhibbu alkafireena

46.author: Ahmed Ali
46.text: Among His signs are the breezes He sends as harbingers of happy news, so that He may allow you to taste of His mercy, and that ships may sail by His command, and you may seek of His bounty, and may haply be grateful.

46.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
46.text: And among His signs is that He sends winds heralding glad tidings, to make you taste His mercy, and so that the ships may sail by His command, and so that you may seek His munificence, and for you to give thanks.

46.author: Arberry
46.text: And of His signs is that He looses the winds, bearing good tidings and that He may let you taste of His mercy, and that the ships may run at His commandment, and that you may seek His bounty; haply so you will be thankful.

46.author: Daryabadi
46.text: And of His signs is that he sendeth winds heralding rain and that he may make you taste of His mercy, and that the ships may sail at His command and that ye may seek His grace, and that haply ye may return thanks.

46.author: Hilali & Khan
46.text: And among His Signs is this, that He sends the winds as glad tidings, giving you a taste of His Mercy (i.e. rain), and that the ships may sail at His Command, and that you may seek of His Bounty, in order that you may be thankful.

46.author: Itani
46.text: And of His signs is that He sends the winds bearing good news, to give you a taste of His mercy, and so that the ships may sail by His command, and so that you may seek of His bounty, and so that you may give thanks.

46.author: Maududi
46.text: And of His Signs is that He sends winds to herald good tidings and that He may give you a taste of His Mercy, and that ships may sail at His bidding, and you may seek His Bounty and give thanks to Him.

46.author: Mubarakpuri
46.text: And among His signs is this that He sends the winds as glad tidings, giving you a taste of His mercy, and that the ships may sail at His command, and that you may seek of His bounty, in order that you may be thankful.

46.author: Pickthall
46.text: And of His signs is this: He sendeth herald winds to make you taste His mercy, and that the ships may sail at His command, and that ye may seek his favour, and that haply ye may be thankful.

46.author: Qarai
46.text: And of His signs is that He sends the winds as bearers of good news and to let you taste of His mercy, and that the ships may sail by His command, and that you may seek of His bounty, and so that you may give [Him] thanks.

46.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
46.text: And of His signs is that He looses the winds as bearers of glad tidings, so that He lets you taste His Mercy and that the ships may sail at His command in order that you can seek His bounty and be thankful.

46.author: Saheeh International
46.text: And of His signs is that He sends the winds as bringers of good tidings and to let you taste His mercy and so the ships may sail at His command and so you may seek of His bounty, and perhaps you will be grateful.

46.author: Sarwar
46.text: Some evidence of His existence is His sending the glad-news-bearing winds so that He would let you to receive His mercy, cause the ships to sail by His command, and let you seek His favor so that perhaps you would give Him thanks.

46.author: Shakir
46.text: And one of His signs is that He sends forth the winds bearing good news, and that He may make your taste of His mercy, and that the ships may run by His command, and that you may seek of His grace, and that you may be grateful.

46.author: Wahiduddin Khan
46.text: Among His signs is this: He sends out the winds bearing good news so that He may make you taste His mercy, and ships sail at His command, so that you may seek His bounty, and be grateful.

46.author: Yusuf Ali
46.text: Among His Signs is this, that He sends the Winds, as heralds of Glad Tidings, giving you a taste of His (Grace and) Mercy,- that the ships may sail (majestically) by His Command and that ye may seek of His Bounty: in order that ye may be grateful.

46.author: Transliteration
46.text: Wamin ayatihi an yursila alrriyaha mubashshiratin waliyutheeqakum min rahmatihi walitajriya alfulku biamrihi walitabtaghoo min fadlihi walaAAallakum tashkuroona

47.author: Ahmed Ali
47.text: Verily We sent many apostles before you to their people, who brought clear signs with them. Then We retributed those who were sinful. It is a duty incumbent on Us to help the believers.

47.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
47.text: And indeed We sent several Noble Messengers before you, to their nations – so they came to them with clear signs – We therefore took revenge from the guilty; and it is incumbent upon Our mercy, to help the Muslims.

47.author: Arberry
47.text: Indeed, We sent before thee Messengers unto their people, and they brought them the clear signs; then We took vengeance upon those who sinned; and it was ever a duty incumbent upon Us, to help the believers.

47.author: Daryabadi
47.text: And assuredly We sent apostles before thee unto their people. They brought them manifest signs. Then We took vengeance upon those who transgressed. And incumbent upon us was avengement of the believers.

47.author: Hilali & Khan
47.text: And indeed We did send Messengers before you (O Muhammad SAW) to their own peoples. They came to them with clear proofs, then, We took vengeance on those who committed crimes (disbelief, setting partners in worship with Allah, sins, etc.), and (as for) the believers it was incumbent upon Us to help (them).

47.author: Itani
47.text: Before you, We sent messengers to their people. They came to them with clear proofs. Then We took revenge on those who sinned. It is incumbent on Us to help the believers.

47.author: Maududi
47.text: We sent Messengers before you to their respective nations, and they brought Clear Signs to them. Then We took vengeance upon those who acted wickedly. It was incumbent on Us to come to the aid of the believers.

47.author: Mubarakpuri
47.text: And indeed We did send Messengers before you to their own peoples. They came to them with clear proofs, then, We took vengeance on those who committed crimes; and it was incumbent upon Us to help the believers.

47.author: Pickthall
47.text: Verily We sent before thee (Muhammad) messengers to their own folk. Then we took vengeance upon those who were guilty (in regard to them). To help believers is ever incumbent upon Us.

47.author: Qarai
47.text: Certainly We sent apostles to their people before you, and they brought them manifest proofs. Then We took vengeance upon those who were guilty, and it was a must for Us to help the faithful.

47.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
47.text: Before you We sent other Messengers to their people; and they came with clear signs. We took revenge upon the sinners, and it was an incumbent duty upon Us to give victory to the believers.

47.author: Saheeh International
47.text: And We have already sent messengers before you to their peoples, and they came to them with clear evidences; then We took retribution from those who committed crimes, and incumbent upon Us was support of the believers.

47.author: Sarwar
47.text: (Muhammad), We had sent before you Our Messengers to their people. The Messengers showed them clear miracles and We took revenge on the criminals. It was necessary for Us to help the believers.

47.author: Shakir
47.text: And certainly We sent before you apostles to their people, so they came to them with clear arguments, then We gave the punishment to those who were guilty; and helping the believers is ever incumbent on Us.

47.author: Wahiduddin Khan
47.text: Surely, We sent messengers before you to their own people, and they brought them clear signs. Then We took vengeance on the guilty. It was certainly Our duty to help the believers.

47.author: Yusuf Ali
47.text: We did indeed send, before thee, messengers to their (respective) peoples, and they came to them with Clear Signs: then, to those who transgressed, We meted out Retribution: and it was due from Us to aid those who believed.

47.author: Transliteration
47.text: Walaqad arsalna min qablika rusulan ila qawmihim fajaoohum bialbayyinati faintaqamna mina allatheena ajramoo wakana haqqan AAalayna nasru almumineena

48.author: Ahmed Ali
48.text: It is God who sends the breezes that raise clouds, then spreads them over the sky as He please, fold on fold, then you see the drops of rain issue from between them. When He sends it down to those of His creatures as He will, they are filled with joy,

48.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
48.text: It is Allah Who sends the winds raising the clouds – therefore spreads them in the sky as He wills and shatters them – so you see the rain dropping from inside it; so when He delivers it upon whomever He wills among His bondmen, thereupon they rejoice.

48.author: Arberry
48.text: God is He that looses the winds, that stir up clouds; and He spreads them in heaven how He will, and shatters them; then thou seest the rain issuing out of the midst of them, and when He smites with it whomsoever of His servants He will, lo, they rejoice,

48.author: Daryabadi
48.text: Allah is He Who sendeth the winds so that they raise a cloud and then spreadeth it along the heaven as He will and breaketh it into fragments, and thou beholdest the rain come forth from the intestines thereof. Then when He maketh it fall upon such of His bondmen as He will, lo! they rejoice.

48.author: Hilali & Khan
48.text: Allah is He Who sends the winds, so they raise clouds, and spread them along the sky as He wills, and then break them into fragments, until you see rain drops come forth from their midst! Then when He has made them fall on whom of His slaves as He will, lo! they rejoice!

48.author: Itani
48.text: God is He who sends the winds. They stir up clouds. Then He spreads them in the sky as He wills. And He breaks them apart. Then you see rain drops issuing from their midst. Then, when He makes it fall upon whom He wills of His servants, behold, they rejoice.

48.author: Maududi
48.text: Allah sends the winds that stir up clouds and then He spreads them in the sky as He pleases and splits them into different fragments, whereafter you see drops of rain pouring down from them. He then causes the rain to fall on whomsoever of His servants He pleases, and lo, they rejoice at it,

48.author: Mubarakpuri
48.text: Allah is He Who sends the winds, so that they raise clouds and spread them along the sky as He wills, and then break them into fragments until you see rain drops come forth from their midst! Then when He has made them fall on whom of His servants as He wills, lo, they rejoice!

48.author: Pickthall
48.text: Allah is He Who sendeth the winds so that they raise clouds, and spreadeth them along the sky as pleaseth Him, and causeth them to break and thou seest the rain downpouring from within them. And when He maketh it to fall on whom He will of His bondmen, lo! they rejoice;

48.author: Qarai
48.text: It is Allah who sends the winds. Then they generate a cloud, then He spreads it as He wishes in the sky, and forms it into fragments, whereat you see the rain issuing from its midst. Then, when He strikes with it whomever of His servants that He wishes, behold, they rejoice;

48.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
48.text: It is Allah who looses the winds that stir the clouds. He spreads them as He will in heaven and disperses them, so that you can see the rain falling from their midst. When He smites with it whom He will of His worshipers they rejoice,

48.author: Saheeh International
48.text: It is Allah who sends the winds, and they stir the clouds and spread them in the sky however He wills, and He makes them fragments so you see the rain emerge from within them. And when He causes it to fall upon whom He wills of His servants, immediately they rejoice

48.author: Sarwar
48.text: It is God who sends the winds to raise the clouds. He spreads them in the sky as He wants, then He intensifies them, and then you can see the rain coming down from the cloud. When He sends it down upon whichever of His servants He wants, they rejoice

48.author: Shakir
48.text: Allah is he Who sends forth the winds so they raise a cloud, then He spreads it forth in the sky as He pleases, and He breaks it up so that you see the rain coming forth from inside it; then when He causes it to fall upon whom He pleases of His servants, lo! they are joyful

48.author: Wahiduddin Khan
48.text: It is God who sends out the winds so that they raise the clouds. Then He spreads them in the sky as He wills and places them layer upon layer and you see the rain issuing forth from their midst. When He causes it to fall on whichever of His servants He pleases, behold! they rejoice;

48.author: Yusuf Ali
48.text: It is Allah Who sends the Winds, and they raise the Clouds: then does He spread them in the sky as He wills, and break them into fragments, until thou seest rain-drops issue from the midst thereof: then when He has made them reach such of his servants as He wills behold, they do rejoice!-

48.author: Transliteration
48.text: Allahu allathee yursilu alrriyaha fatutheeru sahaban fayabsutuhu fee alssamai kayfa yashao wayajAAaluhu kisafan fatara alwadqa yakhruju min khilalihi faitha asaba bihi man yashao min AAibadihi itha hum yastabshiroona

49.author: Ahmed Ali
49.text: Although before it came down they were despondent.

49.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
49.text: Although they had lost hope before it was sent down upon them.

49.author: Arberry
49.text: although before it was sent down on them before that they had been in despair.

49.author: Daryabadi
49.text: Even though before it was sent down upon them, before that, they were surely despairing.

49.author: Hilali & Khan
49.text: And verily before that (rain), just before it was sent down upon them, they were in despair!

49.author: Itani
49.text: Although they were before this—before it was sent down upon them—in despair.

49.author: Maududi
49.text: although before that they were given to despair.

49.author: Mubarakpuri
49.text: And verily, before that -- just before it was sent down upon them -- they were in despair!

49.author: Pickthall
49.text: Though before that, even before it was sent down upon them, they were in despair.

49.author: Qarai
49.text: and indeed they had been despondent earlier, before it was sent down upon them.

49.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
49.text: though before its coming they had despaired.

49.author: Saheeh International
49.text: Although they were, before it was sent down upon them - before that, in despair.

49.author: Sarwar
49.text: at the rainfall, though before that they had been in despair.

49.author: Shakir
49.text: Though they were before this, before it was sent down upon them, confounded in sure despair.

49.author: Wahiduddin Khan
49.text: though before that, before it was sent down upon them, they were in despair.

49.author: Yusuf Ali
49.text: Even though, before they received (the rain) - just before this - they were dumb with despair!

49.author: Transliteration
49.text: Wain kanoo min qabli an yunazzala AAalayhim min qablihi lamubliseena

50.author: Ahmed Ali
50.text: So consider the signs of His benevolence: How He quickens the earth after it had become waste. He is verily the one who will raise the dead. He has power over everything.

50.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
50.text: Therefore observe the result of Allah’s mercy, how He revives the earth after its death; He will indeed resurrect the dead; and He is Able to do all things.

50.author: Arberry
50.text: So behold the marks of God's mercy, how He quickens the earth after it was dead; surely He is the quickener of the dead, and He is powerful over everything.

50.author: Daryabadi
50.text: Look then at the effects of Allah's mercy: what wise He quickeneth the earth after the death thereof. Verily. He is the Quickener of the dead, and He is over every-thing potent.

50.author: Hilali & Khan
50.text: Look then at the effects (results) of Allah's Mercy, how He revives the earth after its death. Verily! That (Allah) Who revived the earth after its death shall indeed raise the dead (on the Day of Resurrection), and He is Able to do all things.

50.author: Itani
50.text: So observe the effects of God’s mercy—how He revives the earth after it was dead. Indeed, He is the Reviver of the dead. He is Capable of everything.

50.author: Maududi
50.text: See, then, the tokens of Allah's Mercy: how He revives the earth after it is dead. Verily He is the One Who will revive the dead. He has power over everything.

50.author: Mubarakpuri
50.text: Look then at the effects of Allah's mercy, how He revives the earth after its death. Verily, that shall indeed raise the dead, and He is able to do all things.

50.author: Pickthall
50.text: Look, therefore, at the prints of Allah's mercy (in creation): how He quickeneth the earth after her death. Lo! He verily is the Quickener of the Dead, and He is Able to do all things.

50.author: Qarai
50.text: So observe the effects of Allah’s mercy: how He revives the earth after its death! Indeed He is the reviver of the dead, and He has power over all things.

50.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
50.text: Look then at the marks of Mercy of Allah; how He revives the earth after its death. He is the Reviver of the dead. He has power over all things.

50.author: Saheeh International
50.text: So observe the effects of the mercy of Allah - how He gives life to the earth after its lifelessness. Indeed, that [same one] will give life to the dead, and He is over all things competent.

50.author: Sarwar
50.text: Look at the traces of the mercy of God, how He has revived the dead earth. God revives the dead; He has power over all things.

50.author: Shakir
50.text: Look then at the signs of Allah's mercy, how He gives life to the earth after its death, most surely He will raise the dead to life; and He has power over all things.

50.author: Wahiduddin Khan
50.text: Look, therefore, at the signs of God's mercy; how He resurrects the earth after its death. Truly, the same God will resurrect the dead; for He has power over all things.

50.author: Yusuf Ali
50.text: Then contemplate (O man!) the memorials of Allah's Mercy!- how He gives life to the earth after its death: verily the same will give life to the men who are dead: for He has power over all things.

50.author: Transliteration
50.text: Faonthur ila athari rahmati Allahi kayfa yuhyee alarda baAAda mawtiha inna thalika lamuhyee almawta wahuwa AAala kulli shayin qadeerun

51.author: Ahmed Ali
51.text: If We send a (blighting) wind and they see (the earth) seared autumnal, they would surely become ungrateful.

51.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
51.text: And if We send a wind and they see their fields yellow because of it, then indeed they would become ungrateful after it.

51.author: Arberry
51.text: But if We loose a wind, and they see it growing yellow, they remain after that unbelievers.

51.author: Daryabadi
51.text: And if We send a wind, and they should see their tilth yellow, then they would thereafter remain disbelieving.

51.author: Hilali & Khan
51.text: And if We send a wind [which would spoil the green growth (tilth) brought up by the previous rain], and they see (their tilth) turn yellow, behold, they then after their being glad, would become unthankful (to their Lord Allah as) disbelievers.

51.author: Itani
51.text: But if We send a wind, and they see it turning things yellow, they would continue thereafter to disbelieve.

51.author: Maududi
51.text: But if We were to send a wind and then their tilth has become yellow, they would never cease to disbelieve.

51.author: Mubarakpuri
51.text: And if We send a wind, and they see it turn yellow -- behold, they then would become unthankful (disbelievers).

51.author: Pickthall
51.text: And if We sent a wind and they beheld it yellow, they verily would still continue in their disbelief.

51.author: Qarai
51.text: And if We send a wind and they see it turn yellow, they will surely become ungrateful after that.

51.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
51.text: Yet if We sent a wind so they see it yellow, indeed after that they would still be unbelievers.

51.author: Saheeh International
51.text: But if We should send a [bad] wind and they saw [their crops] turned yellow, they would remain thereafter disbelievers.

51.author: Sarwar
51.text: Even if We had sent the wind and caused (the plants) to turn yellow and to fade away, they would still have remained in disbelief.

51.author: Shakir
51.text: And if We send a wind and they see it to be yellow, they would after that certainly continue to disbelieve

51.author: Wahiduddin Khan
51.text: Yet if We send a wind and they see their harvest turn yellow, they will then begin to deny [Our favours].

51.author: Yusuf Ali
51.text: And if We (but) send a Wind from which they see (their tilth) turn yellow,- behold, they become, thereafter, Ungrateful (Unbelievers)!

51.author: Transliteration
51.text: Walain arsalna reehan faraawhu musfarran lathalloo min baAAdihi yakfuroona

52.author: Ahmed Ali
52.text: You cannot make the dead to listen nor the deaf hear the call when they have turned back and retreated,

52.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
52.text: For you do not make the dead hear nor make the deaf hear the call when they flee, turning back. (The disbelievers are referred to as dead and deaf.)

52.author: Arberry
52.text: Thou shalt not make the dead to hear, neither shalt thou make the deaf to hear the call when they turn about, retreating.

52.author: Daryabadi
52.text: So verily thou canst not make the dead hear, nor canst thou make the deaf hear the call when they turn away in flight.

52.author: Hilali & Khan
52.text: So verily, you (O Muhammad SAW) cannot make the dead to hear (i.e. the disbelievers, etc.), nor can you make the deaf to hear the call, when they show their backs, turning away.

52.author: Itani
52.text: You cannot make the dead hear, nor can you make the deaf hear the call when they turn away.

52.author: Maududi
52.text: (O Prophet), you cannot make the dead hear, nor can you make the deaf hear your call when they turn back in retreat,

52.author: Mubarakpuri
52.text: So verily, you cannot make the dead to hear, nor can you make the deaf to hear the call, when they show their backs and turn away.

52.author: Pickthall
52.text: For verily thou (Muhammad) canst not make the dead to hear, nor canst thou make the deaf to hear the call when they have turned to flee.

52.author: Qarai
52.text: Indeed you cannot make the dead hear, nor can you make the deaf hear the call when they turn their backs [upon you],

52.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
52.text: You cannot make the dead hear you, nor can you make the deaf hear the call when they retreat, turning about.

52.author: Saheeh International
52.text: So indeed, you will not make the dead hear, nor will you make the deaf hear the call when they turn their backs, retreating.

52.author: Sarwar
52.text: You cannot make the dead listen, nor the deaf hear. Thus, they, (the disbelievers), turn away on their heels.

52.author: Shakir
52.text: For surely you cannot, make the dead to hear and you cannot make the deaf to hear the call, when they turn back and

52.author: Wahiduddin Khan
52.text: You [Prophet] cannot make the dead hear and you cannot make the deaf hear your call when they turn their backs and leave;

52.author: Yusuf Ali
52.text: So verily thou canst not make the dead to hear, nor canst thou make the deaf to hear the call, when they show their backs and turn away.

52.author: Transliteration
52.text: Fainnaka la tusmiAAu almawta wala tusmiAAu alssumma aldduAAaa itha wallaw mudbireena

53.author: Ahmed Ali
53.text: Nor can you make the blind see the way when they have gone astray. You can make none hear except those who believe Our signs and have come to submission.

53.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
53.text: Nor do you guide the blind out of their error; you only make those hear who believe in Our signs, so they have submitted. (The disbelievers are referred to as blind.)

53.author: Arberry
53.text: Thou shalt not guide the blind out of their error neither shalt thou make any to hear except for such as believe in Our signs, and so surrender.

53.author: Daryabadi
53.text: Nor canst thou be a guide to the blind out of their error; thou canst make none to hear save those who believe in Our signs, and who have surrendered themselves.

53.author: Hilali & Khan
53.text: And you (O Muhammad SAW) cannot guide the blind from their straying; you can make to hear only those who believe in Our Ayat (proofs, evidences, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.), and have submitted to Allah in Islam (as Muslims).

53.author: Itani
53.text: Nor can you guide the blind out of their error. You can make hear only those who believe in Our signs, and so have submitted.

53.author: Maududi
53.text: nor can you guide the blind out of their error. You can make none hear (your call) except those who believe in Our Signs and have surrendered themselves (to Him).

53.author: Mubarakpuri
53.text: And you cannot guide the blind from their straying; you can make to hear only those who believe in Our Ayat, and have submitted (to Allah in Islam).

53.author: Pickthall
53.text: Nor canst thou guide the blind out of their error. Thou canst make none to hear save those who believe in Our revelations so that they surrender (unto Him).

53.author: Qarai
53.text: nor can you lead the blind out of their error. You can make only those hear who have faith in Our signs and have submitted.

53.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
53.text: You cannot guide the blind out of their error. Nor shall you make any to hear except those who believe in Our verses, and are submissive.

53.author: Saheeh International
53.text: And you cannot guide the blind away from their error. You will only make hear those who believe in Our verses so they are Muslims [in submission to Allah].

53.author: Sarwar
53.text: You cannot guide the straying blind. You can make no one listen except those who believe in Our revelations and are Muslims.

53.author: Shakir
53.text: Nor can you lead away the blind out of their error. You cannot make to hear any but those who believe in Our communications so they shall submit.

53.author: Wahiduddin Khan
53.text: just as you cannot lead the blind [of heart] out of their error, you cannot make anyone hear your call save those who are willing to believe in Our revelations, and thus surrender themselves to Us and are submissive to Our will.

53.author: Yusuf Ali
53.text: Nor canst thou lead back the blind from their straying: only those wilt thou make to hear, who believe in Our signs and submit (their wills in Islam).

53.author: Transliteration
53.text: Wama anta bihadi alAAumyi AAan dalalatihim in tusmiAAu illa man yuminu biayatina fahum muslimoona

54.author: Ahmed Ali
54.text: It is God who created you of weakness, then after weakness gave you strength, then after strength will give you weakness and grey hair. Surely He makes whatever He wills. He is all-knowing and all-powerful.

54.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
54.text: It is Allah Who created you weak, then after weakness gave you strength, then after strength gave you weakness and old age; He creates whatever He wills; and only He is the All Knowing, the Able.

54.author: Arberry
54.text: God is He that created you of weakness, then He appointed after weakness strength, then after strength He appointed weakness and grey hairs; He creates what He will, and He is the All-knowing, the All-powerful.

54.author: Daryabadi
