54.text: Allah it is Who created you in weakness, then He appointed strength after weakness, then after strength appointed weakness and grey hair. He createth whatsoever He listeth: and He is Knower, the Potent.

54.author: Hilali & Khan
54.text: Allah is He Who created you in (a state of) weakness, then gave you strength after weakness, then after strength gave (you) weakness and grey hair. He creates what He wills. And it is He Who is the All-Knowing, the All-Powerful (i.e. Able to do all things).

54.author: Itani
54.text: God is He Who created you weak, then after weakness gave you strength, then after strength gave you weakness and gray hair. He creates whatever He wills. He is the Omniscient, the Omnipotent.

54.author: Maududi
54.text: It is Allah Who created you in a state of weakness; then after weakness He gave you strength, then after strength He made you weak and old. He creates what He pleases. He is All-Knowing, All-Powerful.

54.author: Mubarakpuri
54.text: Allah is He Who created you in weakness, then gave you strength after weakness, then after strength gave (you) weakness and grey hair. He creates what He wills. And He is the All-Knowing, the All-Powerful.

54.author: Pickthall
54.text: Allah is He Who shaped you out of weakness, then appointed after weakness strength, then, after strength, appointed weakness and grey hair. He createth what He will. He is the Knower, the Mighty.

54.author: Qarai
54.text: It is Allah who created you from [a state of] weakness, then He gave you power after weakness. Then, after power, He ordained weakness and old age: He creates whatever He wishes, and He is the All-knowing, the All-powerful.

54.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
54.text: Allah creates you weak; after weakness He gives you strength and after strength weakness and gray hairs. He creates whatever He will. He is the Knower, the Capable.

54.author: Saheeh International
54.text: Allah is the one who created you from weakness, then made after weakness strength, then made after strength weakness and white hair. He creates what He wills, and He is the Knowing, the Competent.

54.author: Sarwar
54.text: It is God who has created you weak, then, given you strength after your weakness and caused you to become weak and old after being strong. He creates whatever He wants. He is All-knowing and All-powerful.

54.author: Shakir
54.text: Allah is He Who created you from a state of weakness then He gave strength after weakness, then ordained weakness and hoary hair after strength; He creates what He pleases, and He is the Knowing, the Powerful.

54.author: Wahiduddin Khan
54.text: God is the One who has created you in a state of weakness; then He has granted you strength following [your] weakness; later on He has given you infirmity and grey hairs in place of strength. He creates whatever He wishes; He is the All Knowing and All Powerful.

54.author: Yusuf Ali
54.text: It is Allah Who created you in a state of (helpless) weakness, then gave (you) strength after weakness, then, after strength, gave (you weakness and a hoary head: He creates as He wills, and it is He Who has all knowledge and power.

54.author: Transliteration
54.text: Allahu allathee khalaqakum min daAAfin thumma jaAAala min baAAdi daAAfin quwwatan thumma jaAAala min baAAdi quwwatin daAAfan washaybatan yakhluqu ma yashao wahuwa alAAaleemu alqadeeru

55.author: Ahmed Ali
55.text: The day Resurrection is set the sinners will swear: "We did not tarry more than an hour (and cannot be guilty)." That is how they have always been deceived.

55.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
55.text: And on the day when the Last Day is established, the guilty will swear that they did not stay except for an hour; this is how they keep straying.

55.author: Arberry
55.text: Upon the day when the Hour is come, the sinners shall swear they have not tarried above an hour; so they were perverted.

55.author: Daryabadi
55.text: And on the Day whereon the Hour arriveth, the Culprits Will swear that they tarried not but an hour: Thus Were they ever deluded.

55.author: Hilali & Khan
55.text: And on the Day that the Hour will be established, the Mujrimun (criminals, disbelievers, polytheists, sinners, etc.) will swear that they stayed not but an hour, thus were they ever deluded [away from the truth (i.e. they used to tell lies and take false oaths, and turn away from the truth) in this life of the world)].

55.author: Itani
55.text: On the Day when the Hour takes place, the sinners will swear they had stayed but an hour. Thus they were deluded.

55.author: Maududi
55.text: On that Day when the Hour will come to pass the wicked shall swear that they had stayed (in the world) no more than an hour. Thus they used to be deceived in the life of the world.

55.author: Mubarakpuri
55.text: And on the Day that the Hour will be established, the criminals will swear that they stayed not but an hour -- thus were they ever deluded.

55.author: Pickthall
55.text: And on the day when the Hour riseth the guilty will vow that they did tarry but an hour - thus were they ever deceived.

55.author: Qarai
55.text: On the day when the Hour sets in, the guilty will swear that they had remained only for an hour. That is how they were used to lying [in the world].

55.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
55.text: Upon the Day when the Hour has come, the harmdoers will swear that they had stayed no more than an hour. As such they are deceived.

55.author: Saheeh International
55.text: And the Day the Hour appears the criminals will swear they had remained but an hour. Thus they were deluded.

55.author: Sarwar
55.text: On the day when the Hour of Doom comes, the criminals will swear that they have remained (in their graves) for no more than an hour. They had been inventing lies in this way.

55.author: Shakir
55.text: And at the time when the hour shall come, the guilty shall swear (that) they did not tarry but an hour; thus are they ever turned away.

55.author: Wahiduddin Khan
55.text: On the Day the Last Hour arrives, the evil-doers will swear they have not even tarried for an hour, they have always been deluded,

55.author: Yusuf Ali
55.text: On the Day that the Hour (of Reckoning) will be established, the transgressors will swear that they tarried not but an hour: thus were they used to being deluded!

55.author: Transliteration
55.text: Wayawma taqoomu alssaAAatu yuqsimu almujrimoona ma labithoo ghayra saAAatin kathalika kanoo yufakoona

56.author: Ahmed Ali
56.text: But those who were given the knowledge and belief will say: "You have tarried, according to the Book of God, as long as the Day of Resurrection, and this is the Day of Resurrection, but 'you do not know."

56.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
56.text: And said those who received knowledge and faith, “You have indeed stayed in the decree of Allah until the Day of Restoration; so this is the Day of Restoration, but you did not know.”

56.author: Arberry
56.text: But those who have been given knowledge and faith shall say, 'You have tarried in God's Book till the Day of the Upraising, This is the Day of the Upraising, but you did not know.'

56.author: Daryabadi
56.text: And those who have been vouchsafed knowledge and belief will say: assuredly ye have tarried according to the decree of Allah until the Day of Upraising; so this is the Day of Upraising, but ye were wont not to know.

56.author: Hilali & Khan
56.text: And those who have been bestowed with knowledge and faith will say: "Indeed you have stayed according to the Decree of Allah, until the Day of Resurrection, so this is the Day of Resurrection, but you knew not."

56.author: Itani
56.text: But those endowed with knowledge and faith will say, “You remained in God’s Book until the Day of Resurrection. This is the Day of Resurrection, but you did not know.”

56.author: Maududi
56.text: But those who had been endowed with knowledge and faith shall say: “According to Allah's Record you have stayed till the Day of Resurrection. Now, this is the Day of Resurrection. But you did not know.”

56.author: Mubarakpuri
56.text: And those who have been bestowed with knowledge and Faith will say: "Indeed you have stayed according to the decree of Allah, until the Day of Resurrection; so this is the Day of Resurrection, but you knew not."

56.author: Pickthall
56.text: But those to whom knowledge and faith are given will say: The truth is, ye have tarried, by Allah's decree, until the Day of Resurrection. This is the Day of Resurrection, but ye used not to know.

56.author: Qarai
56.text: But those who were given knowledge and faith will say, ‘Certainly you remained in Allah’s Book until the Day of Resurrection. This is the Day of Resurrection, but you did not know.’

56.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
56.text: But those to whom knowledge and belief have been given will say: 'You have stayed in the Book of Allah (the Protected Tablets) till the Day of Resurrection. This is the Day of Resurrection, yet you did not know'

56.author: Saheeh International
56.text: But those who were given knowledge and faith will say, "You remained the extent of Allah 's decree until the Day of Resurrection, and this is the Day of Resurrection, but you did not used to know."

56.author: Sarwar
56.text: Those who have received knowledge and have faith will say, "By the decree of God, you have remained for the exact period which was mentioned in the Book of God about the Day of Resurrection. This is the Day of Resurrection, but you did not know."

56.author: Shakir
56.text: And those who are given knowledge and faith will say: Certainly you tarried according to the ordinance of Allah till the day of resurrection, so this is the day of resurrection, but you did not know.

56.author: Wahiduddin Khan
56.text: but those endowed with knowledge and faith will say, "Indeed, you did tarry, as God ordained, till the Day of Resurrection, and this is the Day of Resurrection: but you were not aware of it."

56.author: Yusuf Ali
56.text: But those endued with knowledge and faith will say: "Indeed ye did tarry, within Allah's Decree, to the Day of Resurrection, and this is the Day of Resurrection: but ye - ye were not aware!"

56.author: Transliteration
56.text: Waqala allatheena ootoo alAAilma waaleemana laqad labithtum fee kitabi Allahi ila yawmi albaAAthi fahatha yawmu albaAAthi walakinnakum kuntum la taAAlamoona

57.author: Ahmed Ali
57.text: Their excuses will be of no avail to the sinners on that day, and they will not be allowed to beg for favour.

57.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
57.text: So on that day the unjust will not benefit from their excuses, nor will anyone ask them for compensation.

57.author: Arberry
57.text: So that day their excuses will not profit the evildoers, nor will they be suffered to make amends.

57.author: Daryabadi
57.text: On that Day the excusing of themselves will not profit those who did wrong, nor shall they be suffered to please Allah.

57.author: Hilali & Khan
57.text: So on that Day no excuse of theirs will avail those who did wrong (by associating partners in worship with Allah, and by denying the Day of Resurrection), nor will they be allowed (then) to return to seek Allah's Pleasure (by having Islamic Faith with righteous deeds and by giving up polytheism, sins and crimes with repentance).

57.author: Itani
57.text: On that Day, the sinners’ excuses will not benefit them, nor will they be excused.

57.author: Maududi
57.text: So that will be the Day when the excuses of the wrong-doers will not avail them, nor will they be asked to make amends.

57.author: Mubarakpuri
57.text: So, on that Day no excuse of theirs will avail those who did wrong, nor will they be allowed to return to seek (Allah's) pleasure.

57.author: Pickthall
57.text: In that day their excuses will not profit those who did injustice, nor will they be allowed to make amends.

57.author: Qarai
57.text: On that day, the excuses of the wrongdoers will not benefit them, nor will they be asked to propitiate [Allah].

57.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
57.text: On that Day, excuses shall not benefit the harmdoers, nor shall they be asked to make amends.

57.author: Saheeh International
57.text: So that Day, their excuse will not benefit those who wronged, nor will they be asked to appease [Allah].

57.author: Sarwar
57.text: The excuses of the unjust on this day will be of no avail to them and they will not be able to please God.

57.author: Shakir
57.text: But on that day their excuse shall not profit those who were unjust, nor shall they be regarded with goodwill.

57.author: Wahiduddin Khan
57.text: So on that Day their pleas shall be of no avail, nor will they be allowed to make amends.

57.author: Yusuf Ali
57.text: So on that Day no excuse of theirs will avail the transgressors, nor will they be invited (then) to seek grace (by repentance).

57.author: Transliteration
57.text: Fayawmaithin la yanfaAAu allatheena thalamoo maAAthiratuhum wala hum yustaAAtaboona

58.author: Ahmed Ali
58.text: We have offered every kind of example here in this Qur'an to men. Even then if you bring a verse to them, those who disbelieve say: "You are nothing but a liar."

58.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
58.text: And indeed We have illustrated all kinds of examples in this Qur’an for mankind; and indeed if you bring a sign to them, the disbelievers will say, “You are upon nothing but falsehood.”

58.author: Arberry
58.text: Indeed, We have struck for the people in this Koran every manner of similitude; and if thou bringest them a sign, those who are unbelievers will certainly say, 'You do nothing but follow falsehood.'

58.author: Daryabadi
58.text: And assuredly We have propounded for mankind, in this Qur'an, every kind of similitude; and if thou bringest unto them a sign, those who disbelieve are sure to say: ye are but followers of falsehood.

58.author: Hilali & Khan
58.text: And indeed We have set forth for mankind, in this Quran every kind of parable. But if you (O Muhammad SAW) bring to them any sign or proof, (as an evidence for the truth of your Prophethood), the disbelievers are sure to say (to the believers): "You follow nothing but falsehood, and magic."

58.author: Itani
58.text: We have cited in this Quran for the people every sort of parable. But even if you bring them a miracle, those who disbelieve will say, “You are nothing but fakers.”

58.author: Maududi
58.text: In the Qur'an We have explained things to people in myriad ways. But no matter what Sign you bring to them, those who are resolved upon denying the Truth will say: “You are given to falsehood.”

58.author: Mubarakpuri
58.text: And indeed We have set forth for mankind, in this Qur'an every kind of parable. But if you bring to them any sign or proof, the disbelievers are sure to say (to the believers): "You follow nothing but falsehood and magic."

58.author: Pickthall
58.text: Verily We have coined for mankind in this Qur'an all kinds of similitudes; and indeed if thou camest unto them with a miracle, those who disbelieve would verily exclaim: Ye are but tricksters!

58.author: Qarai
58.text: Certainly we have drawn for mankind in this Quran every [kind of] parable. Indeed if you bring them a sign, the faithless will surely say, ‘You are nothing but fabricators!’

58.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
58.text: In this Koran We have set forth for mankind all manner of examples. Yet if you bring to them a sign the unbelievers will surely say: 'You are but falsifiers'

58.author: Saheeh International
58.text: And We have certainly presented to the people in this Qur'an from every [kind of] example. But, [O Muhammad], if you should bring them a sign, the disbelievers will surely say, "You [believers] are but falsifiers."

58.author: Sarwar
58.text: We have told people various parables in this Quran. Even if you had shown them a miracle, the unbelievers would have said, "You are only the followers of falsehood".

58.author: Shakir
58.text: And certainly We have set forth for men every kind of example in this Quran; and if you should bring them a communication, those who disbelieve would certainly say: You are naught but false claimants.

58.author: Wahiduddin Khan
58.text: Truly, We have set forth for men in this Quran every kind of parable and indeed, if you bring them a sign, those who are bent on denying the truth are sure to say, "You are only making false claims!"

58.author: Yusuf Ali
58.text: verily We have propounded for men, in this Qur'an every kind of Parable: But if thou bring to them any Sign, the Unbelievers are sure to say, "Ye do nothing but talk vanities."

58.author: Transliteration
58.text: Walaqad darabna lilnnasi fee hatha alqurani min kulli mathalin walain jitahum biayatin layaqoolanna allatheena kafaroo in antum illa mubtiloona

59.author: Ahmed Ali
59.text: That is how God seals the hearts of those who do not know.

59.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
59.text: This is how Allah sets seal upon the hearts of the ignorant.

59.author: Arberry
59.text: Even so God seals the hearts of those that know not.

59.author: Daryabadi
59.text: In this wise Allah sealeth the hearts of those who believe not.

59.author: Hilali & Khan
59.text: Thus does Allah seal up the hearts of those who know not [the proofs and evidence of the Oneness of Allah i.e. those who try not to understand true facts that which you (Muhammad SAW) have brought to them].

59.author: Itani
59.text: God thus seals the hearts of those who do not know.

59.author: Maududi
59.text: Thus does Allah seal the hearts of those who have no knowledge.

59.author: Mubarakpuri
59.text: Thus does Allah seal up the hearts of those who know not.

59.author: Pickthall
59.text: Thus doth Allah seal the hearts of those who know not.

59.author: Qarai
59.text: Thus does Allah seal the hearts of those who do not know.

59.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
59.text: As such Allah seals the hearts of those who do not know.

59.author: Saheeh International
59.text: Thus does Allah seal the hearts of those who do not know.

59.author: Sarwar
59.text: Thus does God seal the hearts of those who do not know.

59.author: Shakir
59.text: Thus does Allah set a seal on the hearts of those who do not know.

59.author: Wahiduddin Khan
59.text: In this way God seals the hearts of those who do not [want to] know [the truth],

59.author: Yusuf Ali
59.text: Thus does Allah seal up the hearts of those who understand not.

59.author: Transliteration
59.text: Kathalika yatbaAAu Allahu AAala quloobi allatheena la yaAAlamoona

60.author: Ahmed Ali
60.text: So have patience. The promise of God is surely true; and let not those who do not believe make you relax (your endeavours).

60.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
60.text: Therefore patiently endure, indeed the promise of Allah is true; and may not those who do not have faith make you impatient.

60.author: Arberry
60.text: So be thou patient; surely God's promise is true; and let not those who have not sure faith make thee unsteady.

60.author: Daryabadi
60.text: So have thou patience; verily the promise of Allah is true. And let not make thee impatient those who have no conviction.

60.author: Hilali & Khan
60.text: So be patient (O Muhammad SAW). Verily, the Promise of Allah is true, and let not those who have no certainty of faith, discourage you from conveying Allah's Message (which you are obliged to convey)

60.author: Itani
60.text: So be patient. The promise of God is true. And do not let those who lack certainty belittle you.

60.author: Maududi
60.text: Therefore, (O Prophet), have patience. Surely Allah's promise is true. Let those who lack certainty not cause you to be unsteady.

60.author: Mubarakpuri
60.text: So be patient. Verily, the promise of Allah is true; and let not those who have no certainty of faith discourage you (from conveying Allah's Message).

60.author: Pickthall
60.text: So have patience (O Muhammad)! Allah's promise is the very truth, and let not those who have no certainty make thee impatient.

60.author: Qarai
60.text: So be patient! Allah’s promise is indeed true. And do not let yourself be upset by those who have no conviction.

60.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
60.text: Therefore, have patience. The promise of Allah is true. Do not let those who are uncertain make you unsteady.

60.author: Saheeh International
60.text: So be patient. Indeed, the promise of Allah is truth. And let them not disquiet you who are not certain [in faith].

60.author: Sarwar
60.text: Be patient. The promise of God is certainly true. Let not the faithless make you despair of the promise of God.

60.author: Shakir
60.text: Therefore be patient; surely the promise of Allah is true and let not those who have no certainty hold you in light estimation.

60.author: Wahiduddin Khan
60.text: so have patience [O Muhammad]! God's promise is true; let not those who will not be convinced make you discouraged.

60.author: Yusuf Ali
60.text: So patiently persevere: for verily the promise of Allah is true: nor let those shake thy firmness, who have (themselves) no certainty of faith.

60.author: Transliteration
60.text: Faisbir inna waAAda Allahi haqqun wala yastakhiffannaka allatheena la yooqinoona
